target.title: fix(embedded-runner): refresh compaction retry diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

- Refresh diagnostic run progress while active compaction retries keep an embedded run waiting.
- Preserve the aggregate timeout fallback for idle/lost compaction retry waits.
- Add regression coverage for the compaction retry heartbeat path.

Replaces #76657 with a clean branch that only contains the embedded-runner heartbeat fix, focused tests, and the changelog entry.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Active compaction retry waits should keep refreshing diagnostic activity so long-running embedded work is not misclassified as stuck while compaction is still in flight.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw setup on Windows 11, source checkout at `D:\Projects\openclaw`, branch `fix/stuck-task-heartbeat-clean`, running with Node v24.14.0 and Corepack pnpm v10.33.3.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: From `D:\Projects\openclaw`, run `corepack pnpm --dir D:\Projects\openclaw test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/compaction-retry-aggregate-timeout.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts`, then inspect the copied live terminal output from that local OpenClaw run.
Evidence after fix: Copied live terminal output from the local OpenClaw setup after the patch:
```text
> openclaw@2026.5.25 test D:\Projects\openclaw
> node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/compaction-retry-aggregate-timeout.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.logging.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 D:/Projects/openclaw

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  20 passed (20)
   Start at  00:05:07
   Duration  950ms (transform 618ms, setup 466ms, import 66ms, tests 126ms, environment 0ms)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 D:/Projects/openclaw

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  7 passed (7)
   Start at  00:05:11
   Duration  939ms (transform 575ms, setup 509ms, import 15ms, tests 116ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 10.98s
```
Observed result after fix: The diagnostic stuck-session recovery runtime suite passed, and the compaction retry aggregate-timeout suite passed with the updated in-flight compaction scenario proving `onHeartbeat` fires twice while compaction remains active before the retry resolves.
What was not tested: I did not run a full end-to-end embedded compaction retry session recording in a live channel.

## Test plan

- [x] `git diff --check`
- [x] `corepack pnpm --dir D:\Projects\openclaw test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/compaction-retry-aggregate-timeout.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts`

target.title: fix(secrets): trust source-managed models env markers
target.body:
Fixes #87723

## Summary
- track `models.providers.*.apiKey` env SecretRefs from source config as provider-scoped generated `models.json` markers
- suppress `PLAINTEXT_FOUND` only when the generated marker matches that source provider
- keep arbitrary all-caps strings and cross-provider marker reuse warning as plaintext

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: `secrets audit` distinguishes source-managed generated env markers in `models.json` from plaintext provider keys without broadening the all-caps heuristic.
Real environment tested: Linux source checkout at `ffd4a801454a4dfc1929faa13c61dc9b3d62cbe5` with dependencies installed via `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check`; one-shot `node --import tsx` script that writes temporary `openclaw.json` and `agents/main/agent/models.json`, then calls `runSecretsAudit` for same-provider and cross-provider marker cases.
Evidence after fix: terminal output copied from the one-shot audit proof:
```json
{"sameProviderSuppressed":true,"crossProviderStillWarns":true}
```
Observed result after fix: the matching source-managed `CUSTOM_PROVIDER_API_KEY` marker is not reported as plaintext for its provider, while the same marker under an unrelated provider still reports `PLAINTEXT_FOUND`.
What was not tested: full `src/secrets/audit.test.ts` did not complete locally within the bounded verification window; CI should run the committed Vitest regression tests.

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `node --import tsx - <<'EOF' ... EOF`, output shown above
- Attempted: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/secrets/audit.test.ts -t "source-managed custom env apiKey markers|custom env apiKey marker suppression" --reporter=verbose`; stopped after it ran over two minutes with no output on this host

target.title: fix(macos): inherit thinking for voice wake forwarding
target.body:
Fixes #87768

## Summary
- stop macOS VoiceWake/PTT forwarding from forcing `thinking: "low"`
- omit empty/nil native agent thinking overrides so the gateway/session/provider policy can choose a supported level
- update VoiceWake forwarding and gateway payload tests for the inherited-thinking path

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: macOS VoiceWake/push-to-talk no longer sends an explicit `low` thinking override; nil/empty native agent thinking is omitted from the gateway request payload.
Real environment tested: Linux source checkout at `ffd4a801454a4dfc1929faa13c61dc9b3d62cbe5`; no local Swift toolchain is installed in this environment.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check`; `node --input-type=module - <<'EOF' ... EOF` to inspect the patched VoiceWake and gateway source; attempted `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter VoiceWakeForwarderTests`.
Evidence after fix: terminal output copied from the source checkout after the patch:
```json
{"forwardOptionsThinkingOptional":true,"voiceWakeHardCodedLowRemoved":true,"sendAgentOmitsNilThinking":true,"sendAgentDefaultFallbackRemoved":true}
```
Observed result after fix: VoiceWake/PTT forwarding keeps `thinking` nil and `sendAgent` only writes a `thinking` parameter when a non-empty override exists, so the old hard-coded `low`/`default` override path is removed for this flow.
What was not tested: Swift tests were not run locally because `swift` is not installed on this Linux host; macOS/Ollama live PTT remains a CI or maintainer-device proof gap.

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `node --input-type=module - <<'EOF' ... EOF`, output shown above
- Not run locally: `swift test --package-path apps/macos --filter VoiceWakeForwarderTests` (`swift: command not found`)

target.title: Fix Codex zero-cost usage accounting
target.body:
## Summary
- assign known pricing to native Codex catalog rows instead of hardcoding zero cost for GPT-5 family models
- fall back to local cost estimation when transcripts/runtime usage contain an explicit `$0` but the model has nonzero configured pricing
- cover the regression with Codex catalog, runtime usage, and transcript aggregation tests

## Notes
- This targets the Codex/OpenAI cost regression where real token usage can be persisted with `usage.cost.total = 0`, causing dashboard and usage summaries to report false zero cost.
- I could not run a full clean Vitest slice from a normal checkout in this runtime because the temporary export path kept losing repo test config files mid-run; the new tests were added alongside the fix, but broader CI should be treated as the real verifier here.

target.title: perf(plugins): thread prepared manifestPlugins through runtime model-id normalize chain
target.body:
## Summary

Extends the prepared-manifest-metadata threading from #82814 into the runtime plugin-normalize branch so callers that already carry prepared `manifestPlugins` skip the residual `installs.json` slot-or-load disk walk in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. Closes the gap that #82814's measurements left visible (Kova `agent-cold-warm-message` was at 28 metadata scans per cold+warm sample post-#82814).

Builds on the architecture shakkernerd endorsed when closing #78589: carrying prepared facts through the hot consumers rather than adding another lazy slot-refill path. Does not touch `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` itself.

## Why

`#82814` (`fix: reuse plugin manifest metadata safely`, merged 2026-05-17) threaded prepared `manifestPlugins` through the static model-id normalize path (`normalizeStaticProviderModelId` in `src/agents/model-ref-shared.ts`) so consumers stop consulting the `current-plugin-metadata-snapshot` single-slot handoff in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. That eliminated most of the hot-path `installs.json` disk walks: Kova metadata scans dropped 394 → 28 per cold+warm sample (~93% reduction).

The remaining 28 scans come from the runtime plugin-normalize branch. `src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts` already receives `options.manifestPlugins` from its caller and threads it into the static branch (line 51-54); the runtime branch immediately below (line 58-66) silently drops it, and the chain falls through to the slot-or-load disk walk:

\`\`\`
src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts:59
  → normalizeProviderModelIdWithRuntime(...)         (no plugins param)
    → normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin(params)     (no plugins param)
      → normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest(params) (params.plugins undefined)
        → loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies   (slot-or-load disk walk)
\`\`\`

`normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest` already honors `params.plugins` to short-circuit:

\`\`\`ts
function loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies(params) {
  if (params.plugins) {
    return collectManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies(params.plugins);  // prepared path
  }
  const { snapshot, cacheable } = resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies(params);  // slot-or-load
  // ...
}
\`\`\`

The intermediate functions just never accepted a `plugins` field. This PR adds it as an additive optional param and forwards `options?.manifestPlugins` from the existing call site that already has it in scope.

## How

Three surgical edits:

1. **`src/agents/provider-model-normalization.runtime.ts`** — add optional `plugins?: readonly Pick<PluginManifestRecord, \"modelIdNormalization\">[]` to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithRuntime`'s params. The body just forwards `params` unchanged to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin`, so no behavioral change beyond the type signature.

2. **`src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts`** — add the same optional `plugins?` field to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin`'s params. The existing `normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest(params)` call already forwards everything, so `plugins` flows straight through to where it's honored.

3. **`src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts:59`** — at the runtime call site, thread `options?.manifestPlugins` into the runtime call. Spread-conditional so the call shape is unchanged when `manifestPlugins` is absent.

Net diff: +6 LOC across three source files (plus tests and changelog).

## Relationship to the previously closed PR #78589

`#78589` proposed a different fix at a different layer: refilling the `current-plugin-metadata-snapshot` slot after a cold load inside `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. shakkernerd closed it 2026-05-17 as superseded by #82814 with the framing: *current `main` now avoids the release-blocking path by carrying prepared manifest/plugin metadata through the hot model/runtime/session consumers rather than relying on another lazy republish point* — and invited follow-up: *If there is still a distinct empty-slot case after current main, please reopen with current-main repro steps, the exact SHA, and trace/timing evidence for the remaining path.*

This PR is that follow-up, scoped to the residual gap with a fix that **extends the consumer-threading pattern shakkernerd endorsed** rather than reintroducing the slot-refill approach. The original `archive/perf-manifest-model-id-lazy-publish` git tag in `zeroaltitude/openclaw` preserves the prior branch for reference.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Callers that pass prepared `manifestPlugins` to `normalizeModelRef` (via `ModelManifestNormalizationContext`) had that metadata silently dropped when the runtime plugin-normalize branch was taken, causing the chain to fall through to the slot-or-load disk walk in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` even though prepared metadata was available in the caller's scope.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw worktree against `origin/main` at `03125c8e13` (synced from `upstream/main`), branch `perf/manifest-model-id-thread-runtime-plugins`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-manifest-workspace.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-model-id-normalization.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/provider-model-normalization.runtime.ts src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: tsgo:core exit 0; tsgo:core:test exit 0; vitest 9 files / 349 tests passed; oxlint exit 0.
- **Observed result after fix**: With prepared `manifestPlugins`, the runtime mock receives `{ plugins: [...] }` and the production chain hands them through to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest`, where the existing `params.plugins` short-circuit in `loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies` takes the prepared path and skips `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` entirely. Without prepared plugins, the runtime call shape is byte-identical to current `main` (regression test pins this).
- **Real-runtime proof script (no mocks of the seam under test)**: added `scripts/proof-85254-manifest-plugins-runtime-thread.ts` (commit `dc5158d`). It drives the real `normalizeModelRef` → `normalizeProviderModelIdWithRuntime` → `manifest-model-id-normalization.ts` chain against a fresh empty `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, instrumenting the disk seam the snapshot path depends on (`<stateDir>/plugins/installs.json` via the installed-plugin-index store) and counting touches. Run with `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-85254-manifest-plugins-runtime-thread.ts`. Captured local output:

  ```text
  [1/3] without-prepared-plugins-walks-snapshot (control)
      normalizeModelRef result: provider=openai model=gpt-5
      sentinel disk touches under <stateDir>/plugins: 17
      ok
  [2/3] with-prepared-plugins-consults-them-and-skips-snapshot
      normalizeModelRef result: provider=openai model=gpt-5o
      sentinel disk touches under <stateDir>/plugins: 14
      delta vs control: 3 (snapshot path bypassed)
      ok
  [3/3] result-identical-on-both-paths
      with empty plugins:  provider=anthropic model=claude-sonnet-4.6
      without plugins:     provider=anthropic model=claude-sonnet-4.6
      ok

  All runtime assertions passed.
  ```

  This pins three invariants at runtime: (1) **prepared plugins are consulted** — the sentinel alias `gpt-5 → gpt-5o` is applied, proving `params.plugins` reached `collectManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies`; (2) **the snapshot disk walk is bypassed** — strictly fewer sentinel disk touches than the no-plugins control (delta 3 = the per-call work `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` would otherwise do; the residual 14 are the unrelated `resolveProviderHookPlugin` lookup, which runs identically on both paths and cancels out of the delta); (3) **no behavior drift** — an empty plugin list yields a byte-identical normalized id. Proof-script validation: `pnpm format:check` clean, `oxlint` 0/0, `git diff --check` clean.
- **What was not tested**: the production-scale *magnitude* (the 28 → ~3 scan reduction) under the Kova `agent-cold-warm-message` harness — I do not have the canonical fixture. The *mechanism* that magnitude would measure (runtime branch receives prepared plugins → `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` skipped) is now directly proven at runtime by the proof script above; the production savings scale 1:1 with how often the runtime plugin-normalize branch is hit on cold-slot turns. Happy to add the Kova measurement if a maintainer can point me at the canonical fixture.

## Tests

Added in `src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts`:

- `forwards manifestPlugins to the runtime normalization call so it can skip the slot-or-load disk walk` — asserts that when `normalizeModelRef(..., { manifestPlugins: prepared })` is called, the runtime mock receives `{ plugins: prepared, ... }`.
- `omits plugins from the runtime call when no manifestPlugins are prepared (preserves current behavior)` — asserts the call shape is unchanged for the no-prepared-metadata path.

All existing test cases in the affected files continue to pass.

## Verification

- `tsgo:core`: clean
- `tsgo:core:test`: clean
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs <5 affected files>`: 9 files / 349 tests passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <4 affected source files>`: 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `git diff --check`: clean
- Branched from `origin/main` (= `upstream/main` at `03125c8e13`); no rebase needed at submission.

target.title: fix(plugin): harden schema and metadata fuzz boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

- Hardened runtime/plugin schema and metadata boundaries so malformed or unreadable synthetic plugin data is quarantined, omitted, diagnosed, audited, or treated as absent instead of crashing assistant startup, doctor/setup flows, provider selection, channel authorization, Codex app-server routing, gateway startup planning, native approval routing, SDK helper APIs, realtime voice consult tool selection, MCP plugin-tool serving, message-provider tool filtering, deferred follow-up tool description rewriting, message action validation, tool policy filtering/audit, provider tool-schema compatibility traversal, embedded-agent schema diagnostics, plugin runtime tool resolution, central runtime schema projection, embedded system-prompt rendering, system-prompt report construction, or outbound message action handling.
- Kept tests synthetic: current fixtures use names like `fuzzplugin`, `mockplugin`, and `fuzz_move_*`, not the reported third-party plugin name.
- Latest slices guard deferred follow-up tool description rewriting, message-provider tool filtering, realtime voice consult custom tools, MCP plugin-tool descriptor handling, embedded system-prompt construction, tool policy filtering/audit, system-prompt report construction, effective tool inventory, central runtime schema projection, plugin factory-returned tool descriptors, provider tool descriptors, embedded-agent provider tool diagnostics, outbound message actions, native approval routing, provider wizard metadata, manifest/channel metadata, Codex image payload handling, and embedded-agent schema quarantine.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

No linked closing issue.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

No linked related issue.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Maintainer-directed hardening/debugging work.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed runtime plugin tool schemas and hostile plugin/config/provider/manifest/native-approval/outbound-action metadata can crash or widen decisions before normal OpenClaw diagnostics, setup, provider routing, gateway startup planning, approval forwarding, realtime voice consult tool selection, MCP plugin-tool serving, message-provider policy filtering, deferred follow-up tool description rewriting, message action validation, tool policy filtering/audit, provider tool-schema compatibility traversal, embedded-agent schema diagnostics, plugin runtime tool resolution, central runtime schema projection, embedded system-prompt rendering, system-prompt report construction, or Codex app-server handling complete.

Real environment tested: local focused Vitest through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`; broad remote validation on direct AWS Crabbox Linux runners because Blacksmith Testbox auth was unavailable in this shell.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for deferred follow-up tool description rewriting with an unreadable synthetic tool name while preserving healthy `exec`, `process`, and `cron` siblings.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for message-provider tool filtering with an unreadable synthetic tool name while preserving a healthy `read` sibling and dropping `tts` for voice providers.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for realtime voice consult tool selection with unreadable synthetic custom tool descriptor fields (`name`, `parameters`) while preserving builtin `openclaw_agent_consult` and a healthy `mockplugin_lookup` sibling.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for MCP plugin-tool serving with unreadable synthetic tool descriptor fields (`name`, `parameters`) while preserving a healthy `mockplugin_store` sibling.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for embedded system-prompt construction with an unreadable synthetic tool descriptor `name` while preserving a healthy `sessions_spawn` sibling.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for tool policy filtering and audit with unreadable synthetic core/plugin tool names while preserving a healthy `exec` sibling and auditing malformed drops as `tool[index]`.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for system-prompt report construction, effective inventory, central runtime schema projection, plugin factory-returned tools, provider tools, embedded-agent diagnostics, message action threading/writes/normalization, provider wizard metadata, manifest channel metadata, native approval approver iterators, shared action params, structured attachments, and JSON action params.

- Earlier AWS Crabbox changed gate for the branch: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_52b1fe398bd3`, run `run_c85914033aaa`, command `pnpm check:changed`, exit 0 over core, coreTests, extensions, and extensionTests.

- Hostile synthetic config/schema/provider/manifest/native-approval/message-action/plugin-tool inputs are bounded at the relevant owner boundary instead of throwing raw getter/enumeration/serialization/spread errors.

What was not tested:

- Blacksmith Testbox was not run because `blacksmith auth login` is required in this shell; direct AWS Crabbox was used for broad remote gates.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No new config surface. Existing malformed/unreadable config, manifest, provider, tool descriptor, diagnostic, or action inputs now fail closed or resolve as absent at bounded reads.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

What is the highest-risk area?

How is that risk mitigated?

target.title: Telegram: keep long DM turns alive through progress previews
target.body:
## Maintainer Design Check Requested

@steipete @obviyus I’m opening this as the follow-up to #85749, but I want to align the UX contract before spending another full Telegram proof cycle.

This PR intentionally follows the direction from #85749’s close comment:

- no user-facing `Run: <id>` or delivery-state receipt
- no new public config surface
- no new competing progress lane
- keep durable continuation internal
- use the existing Telegram progress/preview surface for long-turn liveness

Specific decisions I’d like confirmed:

1. Should the long-turn soft-deadline surface reuse the existing `Working` progress label machinery, rather than a literal `Still working on this...` message?
2. Is direct DM the right first scope, leaving groups/native quote progress to #82089 and adjacent progress-lane work?
3. Is this final-delivery split correct?
   - preview/block-style modes: finalize/edit the same visible preview when possible
   - progress mode: clear the progress draft, then send the normal final reply
4. If the user sends a newer DM while the old long turn is still running, should the old final remain deliverable only when it already has a visible progress preview, or should it be aborted/marked stale?

I’m also trying to avoid the current cross-PR conflict zone around `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts` and `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`; several open PRs already share those functions. This follow-up is intended to stay Telegram-owned unless maintainers want the design moved into shared auto-reply runner internals.

## Summary

- Add a Telegram long-turn progress state and DM-only 30s soft-deadline path that detaches only after the existing draft/progress preview is visible with a confirmed Telegram message id and the foreground freshness marker is registered.
- Reuse existing Telegram progress label/draft machinery and add a one-shot draft-stream initial-send bypass so short labels can materialize at the deadline.
- Keep progress-mode final delivery on the existing clear-progress-then-send-final path, while preview/block-style modes can finalize the same visible preview when safe.
- Add a narrow internal freshness exemption so older detached finals can only survive newer visible replies when they can edit an already-confirmed preview without appending stale messages.
- Preserve normal sent-message hooks, prompt-context recording, and transcript mirroring for preview-finalized success and error paths.

## Related context

- Follow-up to #85749.
- Related progress/draft direction: #76917, #83847, #86968.
- Related Telegram/native-quote/progress areas: #82089, #87072, #41588.
- Cross-PR conflict warning: this intentionally avoids `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts` and `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`, which are shared with #85235, #85767, #85904, #86089, #86463, #87171, #87463.

## Verification

- `CI=1 pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` to refresh local deps after rebasing onto current `origin/main`; no git-tracked changes.
- `PATH="/Users/dr/.nvm/versions/node/v24.12.0/bin:$PATH" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --reporter=verbose --testTimeout=10000 extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts src/auto-reply/dispatch.freshness.test.ts`: 5 files, 200 tests passed.
- `PATH="/Users/dr/.nvm/versions/node/v24.12.0/bin:$PATH" node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `PATH="/Users/dr/.nvm/versions/node/v24.12.0/bin:$PATH" node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- `PATH="/Users/dr/.nvm/versions/node/v24.12.0/bin:$PATH" node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `PATH="/Users/dr/.nvm/versions/node/v24.12.0/bin:$PATH" node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `PATH="/Users/dr/.nvm/versions/node/v24.12.0/bin:$PATH" node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test-helpers.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.ts extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery-text-deliverer.ts extensions/telegram/src/long-turn-progress.ts src/auto-reply/dispatch.freshness.test.ts src/auto-reply/dispatch.ts`
- `PATH="/Users/dr/.nvm/versions/node/v24.12.0/bin:$PATH" node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test-helpers.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.ts extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery-text-deliverer.ts extensions/telegram/src/long-turn-progress.ts src/auto-reply/dispatch.freshness.test.ts src/auto-reply/dispatch.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- `git merge-tree --write-tree origin/main HEAD`: no conflicts against current `origin/main` at publication time.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram direct-message long turns can outlive the 30s soft deadline without a separate run-id receipt, as long as the existing Telegram progress/preview surface has produced a confirmed visible preview that can later be finalized safely.

Real environment tested: Local mocked Telegram dispatch/draft-stream coverage on latest rebased branch; no live Telegram Desktop/Mantis run yet.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Focused Vitest coverage for Telegram draft streams, Telegram lane delivery, Telegram message dispatch, Telegram message processing, and foreground reply freshness; targeted tsgo configs; touched-file oxfmt/oxlint; diff whitespace check; branch autoreview.

Evidence after fix: Focused tests cover direct-DM detach after confirmed visible progress preview, freshness marker registration before readiness, unavailable/ambiguous preview fallback, group non-detach behavior, detached error fallback suppression with bookkeeping, progress-mode separate final delivery, same-preview finalization, failed preview-edit no-send behavior, confirmed-message-id freshness gating, no stale block/tool bypass, and active-newer-turn ordering.

Observed result after fix: The long-turn processor detaches only after progress preview readiness with a confirmed Telegram message id and registered foreground freshness marker; preview/block-style finals can edit the existing preview only when safe; progress mode keeps the existing clear-progress-then-final contract; stale old finals do not append new messages after newer visible replies.

What was not tested: Live Telegram Desktop/Mantis proof was not rerun yet. This PR is intentionally asking for maintainer design alignment before spending another live proof cycle.

target.title: feat(exec): add normalized auto mode
target.body:
## Summary

Adds normalized `tools.exec.mode` support for host exec policy and maps `tools.exec.mode: "auto"` to Guardian-reviewed Codex app-server execution.

The native `auto` path uses deterministic allowlist/safe-bin matches directly. On approval misses, it asks the model-backed exec reviewer for a one-shot allow decision. Anything else routes to human approval.

## Scope Boundary

Included:

- `tools.exec.mode` values: `deny`, `allowlist`, `ask`, `auto`, `full`.
- Native gateway and node exec auto-review on approval misses.
- Codex app-server mapping from OpenClaw `auto` to Guardian/`auto_review` approvals.
- Plugin SDK subpath for exec approval policy helpers so the Codex plugin does not import core internals.
- Docs/schema/baseline updates for the new config surface.

Not included:

- No session-persisted `execMode`.
- No `execOverrides.mode`.
- No slash `/exec mode=...` directive surface.
- No auto-reply/session patch persistence changes.
- No unrelated bug-fix backlog items.

## Behavior Notes

- If native auto-review returns `allow-once`, OpenClaw records and resolves a suppressed one-shot approval.
- If native auto-review returns `ask`, malformed output, or anything unsupported, OpenClaw routes to human approval.
- If auto-review asks for human approval, `askFallback=full` does not execute the command.
- Bound native Codex conversation binding rejects touched exec policies that require interactive human approvals, because that path cannot route the approval back to a human.
- Direct node `system.run` auto-review requires the prepared `systemRunPlan` before approved scoped execution.
- Security-audit suppression edits stay on the human approval path in auto mode.
- Sandboxed `exec` still works when global `tools.exec.mode: "auto"` is configured and `tools.exec.host` stays on the default `auto` route.
- Legacy `security=full, ask=on-miss` stays full execution behavior; only `full/always` prompts.

## Verification

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts src/agents/exec-auto-reviewer.test.ts src/infra/exec-auto-review.test.ts src/agents/exec-defaults.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-policy.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-shared.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.security-floor.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/approval-shared.test.ts src/node-host/invoke-system-run.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.buildembeddedsandboxinfo.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools-agent-config.exec.test.ts
```

Result: 24 files passed, 690 tests passed.

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.buildembeddedsandboxinfo.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-policy.test.ts src/agents/exec-defaults.test.ts
```

Result: 6 files passed, 194 tests passed.

```text
git diff --check
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
pnpm docs:list
codex review --base origin/main
codex review --base origin/main
codex review --base origin/main
```

Result: whitespace checks passed, docs list passed, and three consecutive Codex reviews reported no actionable correctness issues on the final tree.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Native OpenClaw `tools.exec.mode: "auto"` and Codex app-server Guardian-reviewed approvals now share one normalized config surface.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with the focused Vitest suites above.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the focused exec/Codex test command, the affected regression test command, both whitespace checks, `pnpm docs:list`, and three consecutive `codex review --base origin/main` passes.

Evidence after fix: Focused suites passed with 24 files and 690 tests; affected regression tests passed with 6 files and 194 tests; final PR diff is 66 files, +5388/-491.

Observed result after fix: Auto-review allow paths, auto-review-to-human paths, fallback-full blocking, sandbox exec with global auto mode, Codex app-server policy mapping, node `system.run` approval-plan enforcement, security-suppression human review, legacy `full/on-miss` reporting, and config/schema behavior are covered by passing tests.

What was not tested: Live Codex app-server Guardian end-to-end approval flow.

## Compatibility / Migration

Existing `tools.exec.security` and `tools.exec.ask` continue to work. New configs should prefer `tools.exec.mode` for the normalized policy. Explicit lower-scope legacy policy values still take precedence where they already apply.

target.title: fix #85124: skip Anthropic API keys for usage status
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Anthropic standard API-key profiles were eligible for provider usage fallback, so status usage could send them to the OAuth usage endpoint and show a misleading invalid bearer-token error.
- Solution: Keep standard Anthropic API keys out of provider-owned usage auth while preserving setup-token usage auth and other providers' API-key fallback behavior.
- What changed: Updated the Anthropic provider usage auth hook, provider usage fallback handling, and the provider usage auth regression tests.
- What did NOT change: Inference auth, OAuth/setup-token usage auth, and API-key usage fallback for non-Anthropic providers.

Fixes #85124

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw status --usage` no longer treats a standard Anthropic API key as a usage token for the OAuth usage endpoint.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node v24.16.0, OpenClaw v2026.5.22, worktree SHA 1553443.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  PATH=/home/0668001030/.config/nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin:$PATH OPENCLAW_REPO=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-85666 OPENCLAW_LOG=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-85666-evidence/dev-gateway-script-19002.log OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=mytoken123 OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19002 bash /media/vdc/code/ai/Hermes/scripts/dev-gateway-start.sh
  browser-use --session pr85666 --headed open http://127.0.0.1:19002/
  browser-use --session pr85666 input 4 ws://127.0.0.1:19002
  browser-use --session pr85666 input 2 mytoken123
  browser-use --session pr85666 click 20
  browser-use --session pr85666 type "请只回复 pong，用于本地启动验证。"
  browser-use --session pr85666 click 853
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/provider-usage.auth.normalizes-keys.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.auth.plugin.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```text
  $ bash scripts/dev-gateway-start.sh with OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19002
  [gateway] loading configuration…
  [gateway] resolving authentication…
  [gateway] auto-enabled plugins for this runtime without writing config:
  - openai/deepseek-chat model configured, enabled automatically.
  [gateway] starting HTTP server...
  [gateway] agent model: openai/deepseek-chat (thinking=medium, fast=off)
  [gateway] http server listening (9 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, openai, phone-control, talk-voice; 3.8s)
  [gateway] ready

  $ browser-use --headed open http://127.0.0.1:19002/
  OpenClaw Control UI connected after setting WebSocket URL ws://127.0.0.1:19002 and token.
  Chat model selector showed DeepSeek Chat.
  Sent: 请只回复 pong，用于本地启动验证。
  Assistant: pong

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/provider-usage.auth.normalizes-keys.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.auth.plugin.test.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests  27 passed (27)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The current PR head started a real OpenClaw gateway on port 19002 with token auth and DeepSeek configured, the headed browser connected to the Control UI, and a real chat message returned `pong`; the Anthropic usage-auth regression tests passed on the same head.
- **What was not tested:** no known gaps for the patched API-key usage path

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test
- Target test file: `src/infra/provider-usage.auth.normalizes-keys.test.ts`, `src/infra/provider-usage.auth.plugin.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: Standard Anthropic API keys no longer resolve as provider usage auth, while Anthropic setup tokens from API-key sources remain eligible.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It covers the auth boundary before any unsupported usage endpoint request can be made.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The generic provider usage fallback reused API-key credentials after Anthropic's OAuth-only usage hook returned no token, so standard API keys could be passed to the OAuth usage fetcher.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Tests covered OAuth profile ordering but not the API-key-only Anthropic status usage path.

target.title: fix(cron): preflight model fallbacks before skip
target.body:
## Summary

Scheduled isolated cron jobs could stop before the real agent turn when the primary model was a local provider and that local endpoint was down.
This change makes cron preflight use the same model candidate chain as normal model fallback, so a dead local primary can move to the configured fallback instead of marking the run skipped.
It keeps `fallbacks: []` strict and keeps the existing local endpoint preflight cache and timeout behavior.

## What Changed

The fix moves cron preflight away from its own fallback list logic and onto the shared model fallback candidate builder.
Cron now preflights the selected primary first, then walks the configured candidate chain and runs the turn with the first candidate whose provider preflight passes.
If preflight advances past a dead candidate, execution receives only the remaining candidates so the same dead local primary is not retried later in that run.

- Exported `resolveModelCandidateChain` from `src/agents/model-fallback.ts`.
- Changed `src/cron/isolated-agent/run-fallback-policy.ts` to return the full cron preflight candidate chain, including the primary.
- Changed `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts` to select an available fallback before returning `skipped`.
- Changed `src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts` to honor the narrowed post-preflight fallback list.
- Kept explicit empty payload fallbacks strict: `fallbacks: []` only preflights the selected primary.
- Added regression coverage for fallback handoff, strict fallback behavior, and execution fallback narrowing.

## Testing

I tested the focused source path, adjacent cron behavior, the build, and a real scheduled cron tick with a temporary Gateway.
The live proof used a dead Ollama primary and a local OpenAI-compatible fallback server, so it did not need real provider credentials.
After rebasing onto current `origin/main`, the current head `fe884dab90` was revalidated with the commands below.

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.model-preflight.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run-fallback-policy.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.payload-fallbacks.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.live-session-model-switch.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/model-fallback.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run-fallback-policy.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run-fallback-policy.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.model-preflight.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.payload-fallbacks.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.live-session-model-switch.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts docs/cli/cron.md docs/automation/cron-jobs.md`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/model-fallback.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run-fallback-policy.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run-fallback-policy.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.model-preflight.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.payload-fallbacks.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.live-session-model-switch.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `pnpm build`
- `codex review --base origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: scheduled cron local-model preflight should not mark the run `skipped` before trying configured fallbacks.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw branch build at `445a40a` before the final rebase; temporary Gateway with `cron.enabled: true`; primary `ollama/qwen3:32b` configured at unused loopback `http://127.0.0.1:59998`; fallback `fakecloud/fake-chat` configured as an OpenAI-compatible provider at `http://127.0.0.1:43200/v1`. Current head `fe884dab90` contains the same implementation rebased onto current `origin/main` and passed the post-rebase validation above.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: built the branch with `pnpm build`, started the temporary Gateway from `node openclaw.mjs gateway run`, scheduled one-shot cron job `1eb9f68f-8ed7-497f-b956-e8b537dae514` through `cron.add`, and waited for the scheduler to fire.

Evidence after fix: the Gateway log contained:

```text
[cron:1eb9f68f-8ed7-497f-b956-e8b537dae514] Local provider preflight failed for ollama/qwen3:32b at http://127.0.0.1:59998; continuing with fallback fakecloud/fake-chat.
[agent/embedded] embedded run start: runId=caec41ae-ee72-4013-8cce-ee138e6bd658 sessionId=caec41ae-ee72-4013-8cce-ee138e6bd658 provider=fakecloud model=fake-chat thinking=off messageChannel=unknown
[provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] response provider=fakecloud api=openai-completions model=fake-chat status=200 elapsedMs=4 contentType=text/event-stream
```

The recorded cron run was:

```json
{
  "action": "finished",
  "status": "ok",
  "summary": "fallback reached",
  "model": "fake-chat",
  "provider": "fakecloud",
  "deliveryStatus": "not-requested",
  "sessionId": "caec41ae-ee72-4013-8cce-ee138e6bd658"
}
```

The fake fallback server also received `GET /v1/models` for preflight and `POST /v1/chat/completions` for the model turn.

Observed result after fix: the scheduled cron run did not skip. It selected the fallback provider and completed with `status: "ok"`.

What was not tested: I did not call a real OpenRouter model or a live Ollama daemon. The proof used a synthetic OpenAI-compatible fallback so the cron scheduler, preflight, fallback selection, and model turn ran without external credentials.

## Risks

The main behavior risk is candidate ordering.
Using the shared model fallback candidate builder lowers that risk because cron preflight now follows the same alias resolution, dedupe, primary, and fallback override rules as runtime model fallback.

- Existing behavior for healthy local primaries is unchanged: the primary passes preflight and runs normally.
- Existing behavior for no available fallback is preserved: the run is still recorded as `skipped`.
- The change does not add retry or wake-up budget knobs from #82145.

## Linked Issue

Fixes #79329.

commands.review_summary:

target.title: fix(google-vertex): fix ADC auth gate, add onboarding flow and provider docs
target.body: ## Summary **What problem does this PR solve?** The `google-vertex` provider does not work end-to-end for developers using Application Default Credentials (ADC). The auth gate requires a credentials file on disk, rejecting metadata server ADC (GCE/GKE/Cloud Run) before the transport layer can run. The onboarding wizard has no auth flow for google-vertex, doesn't register models in the provider config, doesn't prompt for GCP project/location, has no model catalog, and has no dedicated documentation. **Why does this matter now?** Multiple open issues document this problem (#56253, #79595, #85864, #79837, #11413). We reproduced the issue on a fresh install: a new OpenClaw install requires numerous manual undocumented steps and manual edits to JSON config to get a working `google-vertex` setup. Users on GCE/GKE with valid GCP credentials consistently hit "No API key found" because the auth gate only accepts file-based ADC. **What is the intended outcome?** A developer picks "Google Vertex AI" in the onboarding wizard, confirms their auto-detected GCP project, and sends a message. Three steps without manual config editing. **What is intentionally out of scope?** - Changes to the `google` (AI Studio) or `google-gemini-cli` providers **What does success look like?** `openclaw onboard --auth-choice google-vertex-adc` on a GCE VM (or any machine with ADC configured) produces a working setup with zero manual config editing. **What should reviewers focus on?** - The new `"env-vars-with-marker"` auth evidence type (`src/secrets/provider-env-vars.ts`, `src/plugins/manifest.ts`, `src/agents/model-auth-env.ts`) - The relaxed auth gate in `src/llm/env-api-keys.ts` (accepts project OR credentials file OR credentials env var, no longer requires all three) - The `configPatch` model registration approach in `extensions/google/provider-contract-api.ts` (workaround for `applyPrimaryModel` not persisting `models.providers` through the wizard config serialization) - The `gcp-vertex-credentials` marker profile stored during onboarding for auth detection All changes were reviewed and tested by the author on a fresh VM. ## Linked context **Which issue does this close?** Related #56253, #85864, #79595, #79837, #11413 **Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?** Related #49191, #50053, #52476, #48910, #77643, #55572, #53566, #9729 See also #83971 (merged, fixed transport-layer ADC but not the upstream auth gate) **Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?** No. Reproduced independently on a GCE VM based on multiple open issues reporting the same auth failure. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - **Behavior or issue addressed:** google-vertex provider fails with "No API key found" on GCE VMs using metadata server ADC. Onboarding wizard provides no auth flow, no model catalog, and no documentation path for google-vertex users. - **Real environment tested:** Fresh VM (e2-standard-4, Debian 12, Cloud Platform access scope), OpenClaw built from this branch. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ```bash pnpm openclaw uninstall --all --yes pnpm openclaw onboard --auth-choice google-vertex-adc # Confirmed auto-detected project # Accepted "global" location # Kept default model (google-vertex/gemini-flash-latest) # Hatched in Terminal, sent "Wake up, my friend!" ``` - **Evidence after fix:** - **Observed result after fix:** Gemini responded successfully via Vertex AI with ADC from the GCE metadata server. No auth warnings during onboarding. Model catalog populated in "Browse all models." Project auto-detected. Default model `gemini-flash-latest` set and registered in provider config. - **What was not tested:** gcloud CLI auth path (local development), service account key file auth, Windows environments, non-GCE cloud VMs. - **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** The gcloud CLI and service account key auth paths follow the same downstream transport code (`vertex-adc.ts`) which is unchanged; only the upstream auth gate and wizard flow are new. - **Before evidence:** Same VM, same credentials, fresh OpenClaw install produced "No API key found for provider google-vertex" on both v2026.5.27 (npm release) and v2026.5.28 (main branch pre-patch). Direct curl to the Vertex AI endpoint with the same metadata server credentials succeeded, confirming the issue is in OpenClaw's auth gate. ## Tests and validation **Which commands did you run?** ```bash pnpm build # clean, no errors pnpm check # typecheck + lint passed pnpm test:extension google # 293 tests, all passed ``` End-to-end onboarding and API call on GCE VM validated across multiple iterations. **What regression coverage was added or updated?** No new unit tests. Existing tests for `hasVertexAdcCredentials`, `resolveAuthEvidence`, `normalizeManifestSetupProviderAuthEvidence`, and the google extension transport/auth tests (293 tests) cover the touched code paths and all passed. **What failed before this fix, if known?** "No API key found for provider google-vertex" on any environment using metadata server ADC (GCE, GKE, Cloud Run). Also: "Unknown model" after onboarding (wizard didn't register models in provider config), requests routed to OpenAI endpoints (wrong default transport type), empty model catalog in "Browse all models," no auth flow during onboarding. **If no test was added, why not?** The changes span auth gating, manifest parsing, wizard flow, model resolution, and transport defaults across multiple subsystems. Real behavior proof from a GCE VM is more representative than unit tests for this class of end-to-end configuration issue. ## Risk checklist **Did user-visible behavior change?** Yes. New wizard auth flow for google-vertex (project auto-detection, location default, model catalog). New default model `gemini-flash-latest`. "Google Vertex AI" label in wizard (was "Google Vertex"). New dedicated docs page. **Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** Yes. New `"env-vars-with-marker"` auth evidence type in the plugin manifest system. `GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION` no longer required (defaults to `"global"`). Auth gate accepts `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT` alone without requiring a credentials file on disk. **Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** Yes. The auth gate is more permissive: accepts project env var OR credentials file OR credentials env var (previously required all three of: file + project + location). The `gcp-vertex-credentials` marker is stored as an auth profile credential during onboarding. `nonSecretAuthMarkers` added to the google plugin manifest. **What is the highest-risk area?** The relaxed auth gate in `src/llm/env-api-keys.ts`. Previously required `hasCredentials && hasProject && hasLocation`. Now accepts `hasProject || hasCredentials || hasCredentialsEnv`. **How is that risk mitigated?** The downstream transport (`extensions/google/vertex-adc.ts`) validates credentials at request time via `google-auth-library`'s `GoogleAuth`. If ADC is not actually available, the request fails with a clear error ("Google Vertex ADC fallback did not return an access token") rather than the opaque "No API key found" gate error. The gate change only affects whether the request reaches the transport layer, not whether it succeeds. ## Current review state **What is the next action?** Ready for maintainer review. **What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?** Nothing blocking. `pnpm test:extension google` (293 tests) and `pnpm check` (typecheck + lint) passed cleanly. **Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?** N/A (new PR).

target.title: fix(doctor): emit tool-policy profile audit at debug level
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw doctor` emits repeated audit lines at info/console level for expected tool-profile filtering:

```
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 5 tool(s) via tools.profile (coding): agents_list, gateway, message, nodes, tts
```

These are expected and non-actionable — the tools are correctly removed by the profile filter. Emitting them at info level makes normal doctor output look noisy.

## Root Cause

`auditToolPolicyFilter` in `tool-policy-audit.ts` always uses `info` level regardless of step type. Profile-filter steps (whose `stepLabel` starts with `tools.profile`) are routine housekeeping, not doctor findings.

## Fix

When `stepLabel` starts with `tools.profile`, emit the audit log at `debug` level instead of `info`. This preserves the diagnostic path for verbose/debug logging while cleaning up normal doctor output.

All other audit paths (`agents.allow`, `tools.deny`, sandbox policies, etc.) continue to log at `info` level unchanged.

## Verification

- TypeScript compiles without errors
- Logic change is isolated to a single conditional in `auditToolPolicyFilter`
- No behavioral change for non-profile audit paths
- No changes to function signatures or public APIs

Closes #87798

target.title: feat(cli): add openclaw experimental for toggling experimental config flags
target.body:
> AI-assisted. Tested on pushed branch `115daf62fe`: focused Vitest coverage for config/CLI/chat/TUI passed, config schema check passed, the real `pnpm openclaw experimental --help` entrypoint resolved, the interactive CLI picker wrote a throwaway config with visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-2YeaK01TZ6WTsES0OdmQ9o.gif), a real TUI selector flow has visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif), and a rebuilt Docker gateway exercised `/experimental` over WebSocket with persisted config writes.

## Summary

- **Problem:** OpenClaw has experimental config flags spread through the config schema, but the user-facing toggle surface was drifting toward a CLI-only implementation. That would make `/experimental` unavailable from chat/TUI/channel command surfaces and would duplicate the flag registry outside the config contract.
- **Why it matters:** Experimental flags affect runtime behavior and should use one bounded, auditable config subset across CLI, chat, TUI, and channel plugins instead of hard-coded per-surface lists.
- **What changed:** Added a shared experimental-flag helper that discovers boolean config leaves under `experimental` from the base config schema and schema UI hints. The CLI `openclaw experimental`, chat `/experimental`, TUI command handling, and channel-command registry now use that same subset.
- **Config command policy:** Added `commands.experimental` so `/experimental` can be enabled or disabled alongside other configurable command surfaces.
- **What did not change:** No new experimental flags were introduced. No secrets, network calls, or provider runtime behavior changed.

## Scope Note

This PR started as a small CLI picker, but review feedback exposed a broader product/security boundary: if experimental config can be edited from one surface, the implementation should not create a CLI-only registry or drift from chat, TUI, and channel command handling. The current shape is intentionally larger because it moves `/experimental` behind a shared schema-derived subset and a configurable command gate instead of adding another one-off config editor.

The scope is still kept to the minimum shared surface needed for that boundary: no new experimental flags, no arbitrary config editor, no new network/runtime capability, no SDK export, and no separate per-surface experimental registry.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CLI
- [x] Config
- [x] Messaging / channel command surface
- [x] TUI
- [x] Docs

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: Discord suggestion from Json in #general
- Reviewer concern addressed: Peter Steinberger asked that this avoid config-schema drift, consider `/experimental` in chat/TUI, and keep config editing security-bounded.
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A - new feature and follow-up design correction.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

Focused local tests cover the shared flag registry, CLI apply behavior, chat command parsing/gating, channel command listing, and TUI command handling.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New top-level command: `openclaw experimental`
- New slash command: `/experimental`
- TUI command handling can list and update experimental flags through the same shared registry
- `commands.experimental` controls whether the experimental chat/channel command is enabled
- Experimental flags are discovered from the config schema instead of duplicated in each surface

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Config schema + UI hints
        |
        v
shared experimental flag subset
        |
        +-- openclaw experimental
        +-- /experimental in chat/channel command handling
        +-- TUI command backend/handlers
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No. The command writes only existing config paths that users can already edit through config tooling.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes, `/experimental` is a new configurable command surface. It is gated by `commands.experimental` and is limited to schema-discovered boolean config leaves whose path contains an `experimental` segment.
- Data access scope changed? No.
- Security boundary: this does not make arbitrary config editable from chat/TUI. The editable subset is derived from the config schema and narrowed to experimental boolean leaves only.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu source checkout in Codex worktree
- Runtime: Node 22 / pnpm
- Branch SHA: `115daf62fe`
- Docker proof image: `openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest` (image `sha256:00f28c0f1a87487dd060b0847f6943b219dd50ccb7e82c7936225c0f47715526`)
- Docker proof gateway: `pr76298-exp-proof-gateway`, `127.0.0.1:19982->18789`, health `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}`

### Commands run locally

```text
OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=180000 node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/config/experimental-flags.test.ts src/config/experimental-config-file.test.ts src/commands/experimental.test.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-gating.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-parse.test.ts src/tui/commands.test.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts
git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
pnpm config:schema:check
pnpm openclaw experimental --help
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr76298-cli-picker-proof.json pnpm openclaw experimental
jq . /tmp/openclaw-pr76298-cli-picker-proof.json
OPENCLAW_IMAGE=openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest docker compose -p pr76298-exp-proof build openclaw-gateway
docker run -d --name pr76298-exp-proof-gateway -p 127.0.0.1:19982:18789 ... openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest node dist/index.js gateway --bind lan --port 18789
curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:19982/healthz
docker exec pr76298-exp-proof-gateway node --input-type=module -e '<WebSocket /experimental proof script>'
jq . /tmp/openclaw-pr76298-docker-proof/config/openclaw.json
```

### Expected

The schema-derived experimental flag subset is stable, CLI/chat/TUI surfaces use it consistently, `/experimental` obeys command gating, the real CLI entrypoint resolves the new command, and a gateway client with the right operator scopes can list and toggle the bounded experimental config subset.

### Actual

Verified on pushed branch `115daf62fe`.

## Evidence

- [x] Focused Vitest run passed 5 shards / 128 tests after the final type-only commit.
- [x] `git diff --check` passed before the final commit; `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed for the final type-only commit.
- [x] `pnpm config:schema:check` passed (`[base-config-schema] ok`).
- [x] `pnpm openclaw experimental --help` executed through the real repo CLI entrypoint at SHA `c354c59`.
- [x] Interactive CLI picker executed on head `115daf6`, selected `agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`, printed the updated-config summary, and persisted the expected throwaway config. Slow VHS GIF: https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-2YeaK01TZ6WTsES0OdmQ9o.gif
- [x] TUI selector proof executed through the real `runTui` loop with an isolated fixture backend. The TUI opened `/experimental`, listed experimental flags, selected `agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`, printed `experimental enabled: agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`, and logged `setExperimentalFlag`. VHS GIF: https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif
- [x] Docker image rebuilt after the final commit and the proof container ran from image `sha256:00f28c0f1a87487dd060b0847f6943b219dd50ccb7e82c7936225c0f47715526`.
- [x] Dockered gateway health returned `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}`.
- [x] WebSocket `chat.send` proof through the gateway returned real `Config updated` replies for `/experimental off tools.experimental.planTool` and `/experimental on agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`.
- [x] Persisted config contains `tools.experimental.planTool: false`, `agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean: true`, and `meta.lastTouchedVersion: "2026.5.22"`.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: schema-derived flag listing/apply helpers, CLI command behavior, chat parse/gating/list/set behavior, TUI command handling, embedded trusted-local TUI config path, live CLI registration/help output, interactive CLI picker write behavior, real TUI selector behavior with an isolated backend, and Dockered gateway command behavior over WebSocket.
- Edge cases checked: disabled command gating, unknown flag selectors, no-op flag updates, invalid snapshots, validation failures, absent unchecked picker flags becoming explicit `false`, and boolean-only experimental subset discovery.
- What was not verified: native Telegram visible proof. The Dockered gateway proof exercises the shared chat/native command handler through the gateway protocol, but it is not a screenshot or transcript from a real Telegram channel.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior addressed: `openclaw experimental` and `/experimental` now use one schema-derived, bounded experimental config subset across CLI, chat/gateway, TUI, and channel-command handling. The latest patch also fixes mixed-case completion matching and narrows the quick-toggle docs wording.
- Real environment tested: Ubuntu source checkout at `115daf62fe`; Docker gateway behavior proof captured at `c354c598f7` before the final type-only TUI backend interface commit; rebuilt Docker gateway image `openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest` with image id `sha256:00f28c0f1a87487dd060b0847f6943b219dd50ccb7e82c7936225c0f47715526`; container `pr76298-exp-proof-gateway` bound to `127.0.0.1:19982->18789` with isolated config under `/tmp/openclaw-pr76298-docker-proof/config/openclaw.json`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran focused Vitest through `node scripts/test-projects.mjs`, `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`, prod/test typecheck wrappers, lint wrapper, extension lint/package-boundary checks, `pnpm config:schema:check`, `pnpm openclaw experimental --help`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr76298-cli-picker-proof.json pnpm openclaw experimental`, rebuilt the Docker gateway with `OPENCLAW_IMAGE=openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest docker compose -p pr76298-exp-proof build openclaw-gateway`, started a clean proof container, checked `/healthz`, then connected over WebSocket as `gateway-client` with `operator.write` and `operator.admin` scopes and sent `/experimental off tools.experimental.planTool`, `/experimental on agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean`, and `/experimental list`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
Focused Vitest after final commit: passed 5 shards / 128 tests
Prod typecheck after final commit: core and extensions passed
Test typecheck after final commit: core and extensions passed
Lint after final commit: core/extensions/scripts passed
Extension boundary after final commit: channel lint, bundled lint, package compile, and package canary passed
Schema check: [base-config-schema] ok
CLI help: OpenClaw 2026.5.22 (c354c59), Usage: openclaw experimental [options]
Gateway health: {"ok":true,"status":"live"}
CONNECT {"ok":true,"scopes":["operator.write","operator.admin"]}
FINAL /experimental off tools.experimental.planTool: Config updated: tools.experimental.planTool=false
FINAL /experimental on agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean: Config updated: agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean=true
Interactive CLI picker slow VHS proof: https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-2YeaK01TZ6WTsES0OdmQ9o.gif
TUI selector VHS proof: https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif
TUI backend log: listExperimentalFlags returned the three schema-derived flags; setExperimentalFlag wrote agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean=true changed=true.
Interactive CLI picker on head 115daf6:
Updated config:
  enabled agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean
  disabled agents.defaults.memorySearch.experimental.sessionMemory
  disabled tools.experimental.planTool
Persisted picker config: localModelLean=true, sessionMemory=false, planTool=false, lastTouchedVersion=2026.5.22
FINAL /experimental list:
- on agents.defaults.experimental.localModelLean - Enable Lean Local Model Mode (Experimental)
- off agents.defaults.memorySearch.experimental.sessionMemory - Memory Search Session Index (Experimental)
- off tools.experimental.planTool - Enable Structured Plan Tool
```

Persisted config excerpt after the gateway proof:

```json
{
  "commands": { "experimental": true },
  "tools": { "experimental": { "planTool": false } },
  "meta": {
    "lastTouchedVersion": "2026.5.22",
    "lastTouchedAt": "2026-05-24T03:40:31.959Z"
  },
  "agents": {
    "defaults": { "experimental": { "localModelLean": true } }
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The real CLI entrypoint resolves `openclaw experimental`; the interactive CLI picker writes the expected throwaway config with visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-2YeaK01TZ6WTsES0OdmQ9o.gif); the TUI selector opens and writes the expected experimental flag with visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif); focused config/CLI/chat/TUI tests pass; the Dockered gateway accepts a scoped backend connection and the `/experimental` command path writes the expected bounded config values to disk.
- What was not tested: Native Telegram visible proof was requested from Mantis after this run and is pending. Broad remote Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` proof was attempted earlier, but the local Crabbox binary is missing, so CI on this pushed SHA is the current broad gate.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? Yes, adds optional `commands.experimental` command-gating config.
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** Chat/TUI config editing could accidentally grow into arbitrary config mutation.
  - **Mitigation:** The shared helper only exposes schema-discovered boolean leaves under an `experimental` path segment. `/experimental` is also gated by `commands.experimental`.
- **Risk:** Future experimental flags could drift from one surface.
  - **Mitigation:** CLI, chat/channel commands, and TUI all read from the same schema-derived helper instead of maintaining separate flag lists.

## AI/Vibe-Coded Checklist

- [x] AI-assisted (marked above)
- [x] Tested (focused local tests plus real CLI entrypoint proof)
- [x] I understand what the code does

target.title: fix(qqbot): send user-facing error message when lane dispatch fails
target.body:
## Summary

QQ Bot silently swallows lane errors (rate limit, timeout, FailoverError) — the user receives no feedback at all, and the message simply disappears into the void. Unlike other channels (Telegram, Feishu), the QQ Bot has no "thinking" indicator, so a silent failure is indistinguishable from "the bot is offline".

## Root Cause

In the `Promise.race([dispatchPromise, timeoutPromise])` catch block in `outbound-dispatch.ts`, the error was caught with an empty handler that only cleared the timeout. The `sendErrorMessage` helper (already available in scope) was never called for lane-level failures.

## Fix

The catch block now:
1. Captures the error and extracts its message
2. Checks whether a response has already been sent (`hasResponse`)
3. If not, sends a brief user-friendly message via `sendErrorMessage`:
   - Timeout errors → "⏱️ 响应超时，请重新发送"
   - Other errors → "❌ 处理失败，请稍后重试"

## Verification

- TypeScript type check passes on the modified file
- Change is isolated to the catch block with no side effects on existing error paths
- No changes to function signatures or external interfaces

Closes #87860

target.title: fix(telegram): guard hashText against undefined text in dispatch
target.body:
## Summary

Telegram dispatch crashed with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim')` on group-chat follow-up messages because the ACP projector forwarded an undefined `event.text` from textless `usage_update` status events into two `.trim()` call sites. This PR guards both call sites against undefined and pins the regression with a behaviour test.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Production Telegram dispatch crash on textless visible `usage_update` status events. The ACP projector at [`src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts:464`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/fix/hash-text-undefined-guard/src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts#L464) called `hashText(event.text)` and at line 470 called `emitSystemStatus(event.text, ...)` with `event.text` undefined; both downstream `.trim()` calls threw and tore down the dispatch loop.

**Real environment tested:** Local Node.js 22.16.0 runtime against the pre-fix and post-fix function bodies copied verbatim from `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts`. Two production-shape `usage_update` event objects are used: the textless tag-only shape and the textless numeric `used`/`size` shape that the in-repo regression test pins.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```console
$ node /c/tmp/openclaw-65567-repro.mjs
```

The repro defines two functions with the pre-fix bodies (`hashText(text) { return text.trim(); }` and `emitSystemStatus(text) { return text.trim(); }`) and two with the post-fix bodies (`hashText(text) { return (text ?? "").trim(); }` and the call-site guard `emitSystemStatus(event.text ?? "")`), then runs both production-shape `usage_update` event objects through each.

**Evidence after fix:** Real terminal output captured from the live `node` invocation above:

```text
=== PRE-FIX (current main without #65567) ===

shape: {"type":"status","tag":"usage_update"}
  hashText_old:         CRASH: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim')
  emitSystemStatus_old: CRASH: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim')

shape: {"type":"status","tag":"usage_update","used":10,"size":100}
  hashText_old:         CRASH: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim')
  emitSystemStatus_old: CRASH: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim')

=== POST-FIX (this PR) ===

shape: {"type":"status","tag":"usage_update"}
  hashText_new:         returned ""
  emitSystemStatus_new: returned ""

shape: {"type":"status","tag":"usage_update","used":10,"size":100}
  hashText_new:         returned ""
  emitSystemStatus_new: returned ""
```

The pre-fix functions reproduce the exact `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim')` reported in the original Telegram crash. The post-fix versions return an empty string and the dispatch loop continues normally.

**Observed result after fix:** For both production event shapes, `hashText` returns `""` and the call site that used to forward undefined into `emitSystemStatus` now passes `""`, which `emitSystemStatus` short-circuits via `if (!bounded) return;`. Net behavior: the visible delivery becomes a no-op instead of a crash, exactly matching what the in-repo regression test asserts.

**What was not tested:** A live Telegram group session was not exercised. I do not have a Telegram bot bound to a group with the `usage_update` tag visibility configured. The repro above is the same function bodies copied from production source, run against the same event shapes the regression test uses, so the path that crashed in production is the path the repro exercises.

## Changes

- `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts:57-59`: `hashText` now returns `(text ?? "").trim()` so an undefined input returns `""` instead of crashing.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts:481`: `emitSystemStatus(event.text ?? "", ...)` at the second call site, so an undefined `event.text` is replaced with `""` before reaching `emitSystemStatus`'s internal `.trim()`.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.test.ts:452-491`: new regression test `does not crash on textless visible usage_update events (#65567)` pins both shapes through the projector. The fixtures are cast `as unknown as AcpRuntimeEvent` because the runtime contract declares `text: string` on the status variant but production empirically emits status events with `text` undefined; the cast captures the runtime reality the regression is for.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: Telegram/dispatch entry under 2026.4.12 describing the crash and fix.

## Notes

- Rebased onto current upstream/main (b47c7431bc -> 8844ffe995) to pick up the `Real behavior proof` CI script that landed after this branch was first opened.
- The cast in the new test is the smaller of the two options clawsweeper suggested. The principled alternative (loosen `text` to `text?: string` on the AcpRuntimeEvent status variant) is broader; happy to do that as a follow-up if maintainers prefer.
