target.title: fix(moonshot): rewrite duplicate native Kimi tool_call ids on replay
target.body:
## Summary

- Resolve the Moonshot/Kimi duplicate `tool_call_id` HTTP 400 (#51593) by opting Moonshot into the shared occurrence-aware replay sanitiser, **without** adding any Kimi-specific public policy surface — the fix shape Codex review on this PR recommended.
- `extensions/moonshot/index.ts` overrides `buildReplayPolicy` with `sanitizeToolCallIds: true, toolCallIdMode: "strict"`. The shared sanitiser already keeps native Kimi ids intact on first use (`sanitizeToolCallId` returns `functions.<name>:<index>` verbatim in strict mode, satisfying #62319) and rewrites later duplicates via the occurrence seed (`functions.exec:26` → `functionsexec262`), so the duplicate that previously reached Moonshot now gets a distinct id.
- Remove the `preserveNativeKimiToolCallIds` flag an earlier revision threaded through `ProviderReplayPolicy`, `TranscriptPolicy`, the embedded-runner replay sanitiser, and the image sanitiser. Reusing the existing native-id detector keeps a single source of truth and avoids a compatibility-sensitive plugin-facing knob (Codex P1 `src/plugins/types.ts`), and keeps `TranscriptPolicy` literals assignable (Codex P2 `transcript-policy.ts`).
- Out of scope: relaxing the #62319 invariant (native Kimi ids must survive *first* occurrence) and changing tool_call_id generation upstream of the runner.

## Linked context

Closes #51593

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request. The issue is `bug:crash`, `P1`, `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`, `queueable-fix`, `source-repro`. ClawSweeper's Codex review confirmed the bug is reproducible and that reusing the existing occurrence-aware sanitiser (rather than a new flag) is the right fix shape; this revision implements exactly that.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Moonshot/Kimi returns native tool_call_ids shaped like `functions.exec:26`. Because #62319 requires those native ids to survive replay, Moonshot disabled the replay sanitiser entirely (`sanitizeToolCallIds: false`). Long sessions / WhatsApp group chats reused the same `<name>:<index>` slot, replayed the same id twice in one request, and Moonshot rejected the second assistant turn with HTTP 400 `tool call id <id> is duplicated`.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux with Node `v22.22.3`, on top of latest `main` (which carries the refreshed prompt snapshots).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Replayed two assistant turns sharing the same `functions.exec:26` id through the real `sanitizeToolCallIdsForCloudCodeAssist` in strict mode with no Kimi-specific option — the exact transform Moonshot's `buildReplayPolicy` now drives.
- Evidence after fix:

  Moonshot `buildReplayPolicy` (`extensions/moonshot/index.ts`) now resolves to:

  ```
  { validateGeminiTurns: true, validateAnthropicTurns: true, sanitizeToolCallIds: true, toolCallIdMode: "strict" }
  ```

  Replaying the duplicate `functions.exec:26` through the real sanitiser:

  ```
  === AFTER-FIX (no Kimi-specific flag, strict mode) ===
  first  toolCall id : functions.exec:26
  first  toolResult  : functions.exec:26
  second toolCall id : functionsexec262
  second toolResult  : functionsexec262
  first preserved native : true
  second rewritten unique: true
  first pair matches  : true
  second pair matches : true
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The first `functions.exec:26` survives verbatim (so #62319 stays fixed), the duplicate gets `functionsexec262` (unique, alphanumeric via the existing `makeUniqueToolId` strict path), and the paired `toolResult` ids follow by encounter order. The exact wire shape Moonshot would reject is no longer producible.
- What was not tested: A live WhatsApp group chat against Moonshot `kimi-k2.5/k2.6`. The fix sits at the runtime replay sanitiser boundary that produces the wire-level ids, so the smoke exercises the exact transformation the live request consumes; this environment has no Moonshot credentials.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Boundary smoke against the real sanitiser, not a live Moonshot call. The reporter's HTTP 400 capture in #51593 documents the upstream side of the same boundary.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/tool-call-id.test.ts extensions/moonshot/index.test.ts` → all green (tool-call-id 25, moonshot 5).
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json` and `-p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json` → no errors (the earlier `TranscriptPolicy` required-field type regression is gone).
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <touched files>` → clean; `oxfmt --check <touched files>` → all formatted.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/agents/tool-call-id.test.ts`: "preserves the first native Kimi tool_call id and rewrites duplicates" now asserts the behaviour with **no** Kimi flag — locking both the #62319 first-occurrence invariant and the dedup-on-replay fix.
- `extensions/moonshot/index.test.ts`: asserts the replay policy is `sanitizeToolCallIds: true, toolCallIdMode: "strict"` and explicitly `not.toHaveProperty("preserveNativeKimiToolCallIds")`.

What failed before this fix, if known?

On current main Moonshot's policy has `sanitizeToolCallIds: false`, so the sanitiser never runs and duplicate native ids reach Moonshot verbatim — exactly the reported HTTP 400.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes (positive). Moonshot multi-turn sessions / group chats now survive duplicate native id replays instead of failing with HTTP 400.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No (tool_call_ids are rewritten in-flight only; nothing is persisted differently).

What is the highest-risk area?

The Moonshot opt-in flips its replay policy from `sanitizeToolCallIds: false` to `true`. Native Kimi ids are still preserved on first use by the shared sanitiser, so #62319 is unaffected; only genuine duplicates change.

How is that risk mitigated?

No public policy surface is added — the change is contained to the Moonshot plugin's `buildReplayPolicy` plus the existing shared sanitiser. The `tool-call-id` test locks the first-occurrence-preserved / duplicate-rewritten behaviour so future contributors cannot regress #62319.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer / ClawSweeper review. This revision implements ClawSweeper's recommended fix shape (drop the public Kimi flag, keep Moonshot's sanitiser opt-in, preserve first native ids via the existing detector). A live Moonshot group-chat smoke would confirm the rewritten shape end-to-end.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

A live WhatsApp group-chat / long-session Moonshot smoke (needs Moonshot credentials not available in this environment). The `Real behavior proof` gate may need a maintainer `proof:` override for this boundary-level fix.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Codex review (needs changes): removed the `preserveNativeKimiToolCallIds` `ProviderReplayPolicy`/`TranscriptPolicy` surface (P1), kept Moonshot's sanitiser opt-in, preserved first native Kimi ids via the existing detector, kept transcript-policy types assignable (P2), and updated the focused tool-call-id / Moonshot tests.

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the touched code paths, implemented the review-recommended fix shape, and ran the validation commands above.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

Without this PR, zh-CN users see raw English text in those UI areas and inconsistent/wrong translations in existing areas. - **Intended outcome**: zh-CN users get a fully translated, terminology-consistent, and naturally readable Control UI. All new activity strings translated, ~80 existing strings fixed or polished. - **Out of scope**: Other locale files (only zh-CN touched). Structural changes to the i18n system. Adding/modifying English source strings. RTL locale completeness (only format fixed for ar/fa). - **Success**: All 27 activity keys show Chinese text. No English residuals in translated strings. passes clean.`pnpm run control-ui-i18n` ## Linked context - Related: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85778 had translations that were later overwritten by CI locale refresh during merge. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - **Behavior addressed**: zh-CN UI translation accuracy and completeness. - **Real environment tested**: Local fork of openclaw at commit , built and deployed on a self-hosted Ubuntu 22.04 VM (OpenClaw Gateway v2026.5.20), viewed in Edge browser. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ``` cd ui pnpm build # vite build → ✓ 1.40s pnpm run control-ui-i18n # locale check cp -r dist/control-ui/ /control-ui/ openclaw gateway --port 18789 # start → serves updated UI ``` - **Evidence after fix**: ``` zh-CN: clean (fallbacks=0) ``` The 27 fallbacks are pre-existing keys awaiting upstream AI provider validation (no local AI provider configured). All new activity keys are translated and the check detects no issues. - **Observed result**: All changed/dirty keys pass the sync check. No regression in other pending locales. File changed: 1 file, +105/−106 lines. Gateway serves Control UI at with zh-CN rendering correctly.`local18789` - **What was not tested**: Runtime UI rendering across all 18 supported language pairs (only zh-CN verified). Mobile/tablet viewport rendering. - **Proof limitations**: Tested on headless VM — no screenshot capture tool available. Fallback count of 27 is a pre-existing limitation of running without an AI provider on this setup — upstream CI will clear those on merge. - **Before evidence**: The original file had strings like (wrong — light color mode, not sleep), (inconsistent with later usage), (English-only in some keys).`common.light: "浅睡"``cron.runs.delivery: "投递"``dreaming.*: "Dreaming…"` ## Tests and validation - Commands run: ``` pnpm run control-ui-i18n # clean pass cd ui && pnpm build # vite build → 1.40s, no errors ``` - **Regression coverage**: Every modified key was verified pairwise against and checked with the sync script to ensure no sync-side conflicts.`en.ts` - **If no test added, why**: This is a locale-only change — there is no behavior logic to unit test. The i18n check script is the appropriate validation. ## Risk checklist - **User-visible behavior change?** — zh-CN locale text changes. No behavioral or logic changes.`Yes` - **Config, environment, or migration behavior change?** `No` - **Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** `No` - **Highest-risk area**: None. Only string values changed; if any translation is inaccurate, the worst case is a confusing label — no security or data integrity impact. - **Risk mitigation**: Every changed key was verified against the English source for semantic accuracy. Consistent glossary terms were applied across sections. The locale check script confirmed no structural issues.

target.title: fix(telegram): handle vanished claim race in isolated polling spool drain
target.body:
## Summary

`recoverStaleTelegramSpooledUpdateClaims` in `extensions/telegram/src/telegram-ingress-spool.ts` could throw `ENOENT` when a `.processing` claim file was removed concurrently between the directory scan and the final `fs.rename(claimedPath, pendingPath)`. The isolated-polling drain loop catches that throw, logs `[telegram][diag] isolated polling spool drain failed (N): ENOENT: ... rename '...processing' -> '...json'`, and increments `consecutiveDrainFailures`. The drain recovers on the next interval, so no updates are lost, but it produces recurring error noise and spurious failure counters under normal isolated-polling load.

Fixes #87847

## Root cause

The recovery loop reads the spool directory, then for each `.processing` entry stats it, optionally consults `shouldRecover`, and finally either deletes the claim (if a pending file already exists) or renames it back to pending. Two concurrent paths can remove or recreate the claim inside that window — another drain lane calling `claimTelegramSpooledUpdate`, `releaseTelegramSpooledUpdateClaim`, `failTelegramSpooledUpdateClaim`, or a second process running the same recovery:

1. The file vanishes after the scan/stat, so the final `fs.rename` throws `ENOENT` (the reported symptom). The sibling `releaseTelegramSpooledUpdateClaim` already guards its own rename against `ENOENT`/`EEXIST`; this path did not.
2. When `readJsonFileWithFallback` returns `null` (file already gone or unreadable), `parsed` is `null`; the old `parsed && !(await shouldRecover(...))` guard short-circuited to `false`, so no `continue` ran and execution fell into the unguarded rename.

## Fix

- Wrap the recovery `pathExists`/`rename` step in the same `ENOENT` (skip) / `EEXIST` (drop the claim, skip) handling that `releaseTelegramSpooledUpdateClaim` already uses.
- Skip a claim whose payload no longer parses instead of blind-renaming a vanished/unreadable file back to pending (the optional hardening called out in the issue). The time-based stale sweep still recovers genuinely stale claims, and `listTelegramSpooledUpdateClaims` already ignores unparseable claims, so this stays consistent.

## Why this is safe

- The change is confined to error handling at the end of one recovery branch; the happy path (rename eligible stale claims back to pending) is unchanged.
- `ENOENT`/`EEXIST` from the rename mean a concurrent op already finalized the claim, so skipping is the correct outcome — the entry is no longer this loop's to recover. Any other error code still propagates via `throw err`.
- No update is dropped: a vanished claim was already handled elsewhere, and a still-present stale claim is unaffected. `recovered` is only incremented after a successful transition, so the returned count stays accurate.

## Security / runtime controls unchanged

- No change to claim ownership semantics: `isTelegramSpooledUpdateClaimOwnedByOtherLiveProcess`, the process-liveness check, and the stale-claim window (`TELEGRAM_SPOOLED_UPDATE_PROCESSING_STALE_MS`) are untouched.
- No config, default, schema, or public API/SDK surface changes; no new permissions or operator actions. File modes (`0o600`) and the claim/release/fail protocol are unchanged. Behavior is runtime-enforced FS error handling, not prompt-driven.

## Real behavior proof

The defect is a nondeterministic filesystem TOCTOU that emits an internal diagnostic log (not channel-visible message content) and cannot be triggered on demand by a live Telegram message. I forced the exact race window deterministically with a `shouldRecover` callback that removes the `.processing` claim before the recovery rename — exactly what a concurrent drain lane / release / multi-process recovery does in production (`staleMs: 0` matches the isolated-polling drain caller in `polling-session.ts`).

Behavior addressed: `recoverStaleTelegramSpooledUpdateClaims` no longer throws when a claim file vanishes/recreates mid-recovery, so the drain stops logging `[telegram][diag] isolated polling spool drain failed` and stops bumping `consecutiveDrainFailures` for this race.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout (Telegram plugin loads from TS source in the gateway), macOS, Node v24.8.0. The harness uses an ephemeral `os.tmpdir()` spool directory; no tokens, accounts, or personal data were involved, so nothing required redaction.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- Before patch (unpatched recovery): `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/telegram-ingress-spool.test.ts -t "ENOENT race"`
- After patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/telegram-ingress-spool.test.ts`
- Extension lane: `pnpm test:extension telegram`
- Repo changed-file gate: `pnpm check:changed` and `git diff --check`

Evidence after fix:

Before the fix, recovery throws the issue's exact error (temp path is a generated scratch dir):

```
FAIL  Telegram ingress spool > recovers cleanly when a processing file vanishes mid-recovery (ENOENT race)
Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, rename '<tmp>/openclaw-telegram-spool-XXXXXX/0000000000000060.json.processing' -> '<tmp>/openclaw-telegram-spool-XXXXXX/0000000000000060.json'
 ❯ recoverStaleTelegramSpooledUpdateClaims extensions/telegram/src/telegram-ingress-spool.ts:446:7
 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  1 failed | 7 skipped (8)
```

This is the same throw the drain loop wraps as `[telegram][diag] isolated polling spool drain failed (N): ENOENT: no such file or directory, rename '...processing' -> '...json'` while bumping `consecutiveDrainFailures`.

After the fix, the race path resolves cleanly and the full file (plus a second test covering the unreadable-claim skip) is green:

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  9 passed (9)
```

Extension lane and changed-file gate after the fix:

```
pnpm test:extension telegram   -> Test Files 123 passed (123) | Tests 1926 passed (1926)
pnpm check:changed             -> typecheck/lint/import-cycles/dependency+patch guards all PASS
git diff --check               -> clean
```

Observed result after fix: recovery returns `0` (no eligible recovery) and the spool directory ends empty for the vanished-claim case; the unreadable-claim case leaves the `.processing` file untouched without consulting `shouldRecover`. No throw, the drain loop completes normally, and `consecutiveDrainFailures` stays at 0.

## Out of scope (intentional)

- General-purpose garbage collection of orphaned/corrupt `.processing` files beyond the existing time-based stale sweep.
- The `EEXIST` branch mirrors `releaseTelegramSpooledUpdateClaim` for parity; it is hardening for the rename collision case and is not separately covered by a deterministic test (the window is not reachable via `shouldRecover`).

---

AI-assisted change. The fix, tests, and the before/after proof above were produced and run locally on the setup described.

target.title: fix(openrouter): reconcile streamed cost with /generation total_cost
target.body:
## Summary

- Wrap the OpenRouter stream at its outermost layer with a fail-open reconciliation step that, after the chat-completion stream ends, fetches `GET /api/v1/generation?id=<responseId>` and overwrites `message.usage.cost.total` with the authoritative billed amount when it is larger than the streamed estimate.
- Add a fail-open `/generation` client + reconciliation helper in `extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.ts`; compose it as the outermost wrapper in `wrapOpenRouterProviderStream` so it sees the final assistant message produced by every downstream payload-patch wrapper.
- Out of scope: tier-pricing tables (OpenRouter owns those), schema migrations (cost field shape is unchanged), per-call enable/disable config, async/background reconciliation that would touch session storage after the turn completes.

## Linked context

Closes #68066

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request; this follows ClawSweeper's triage that flagged the issue as a `queueable-fix` + `source-repro` + `fix-shape-clear` candidate with the reporter's own Option 1 patch shape (post-hoc `/generation` lookup + overwrite).

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Tier-priced OpenRouter models (long-context Qwen tiers in the report) bill substantially more than the streamed `usage.cost.total` exposed in the final chunk. The OpenClaw gateway previously wrote that base-tier value straight into the assistant transcript, so JSONL session cost totals were a 4× undercount on the reported long-context calls and roughly 50% under at the aggregate-traffic level.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux with Node `v22.22.3`, pnpm `11.2.2` via corepack.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local OpenClaw source smoke with `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" node --import tsx --input-type=module -e '...'` against the real `reconcileOpenRouterUsageCost` helper, exercising each documented failure mode against the four cases ClawSweeper called out (tier-priced long context, short-context parity, HTTP failure, missing responseId).
- Evidence after fix: Copied console output from that local OpenClaw source smoke:

  ```
  === AFTER-FIX BEHAVIOR ===
  Long-context tier-priced: {\"status\":\"updated\",\"previousCost\":0.13342,\"updatedCost\":0.53366} final cost: 0.53366
  Short-context parity   : {\"status\":\"skipped\",\"reason\":\"authoritative total_cost 0.00628 not greater than streamed cost 0.00628\"} final cost: 0.00628
  [openrouter-stream] openrouter cost reconciliation skipped: HTTP 503 from /generation?id=gen-410k-token-call
  HTTP failure fallback  : {\"status\":\"skipped\",\"reason\":\"generation lookup returned no usable total_cost\"} final cost: 0.13342
  No responseId          : {\"status\":\"skipped\",\"reason\":\"missing responseId\"} final cost: 0.13342
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The long-context tier-priced 410k-token call (the exact scenario in the report) now reconciles from the streamed `0.13342` to the authoritative `0.53366` — recovering the 4× undercount end-to-end through the real `reconcileOpenRouterUsageCost` boundary. Short-context parity, HTTP failure, and missing-responseId paths all degrade to the streamed value with a warn-level log instead of mutating the cost.
- What was not tested: A live call against the real `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/generation` endpoint. The fix is at the OpenRouter stream wrapper boundary; the smoke above uses the same boundary helpers with a stub `fetch` to demonstrate the exact assistant-message mutation downstream session storage sees.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The after-fix proof uses the real `reconcileOpenRouterUsageCost` from the source checkout, but does not exercise the network call to OpenRouter because this environment is not configured with OpenRouter credentials. The reporter's evidence in the issue body (six `qwen/qwen3.6-plus` calls, with a 4× ratio at 410k tokens and parity below the tier threshold) demonstrates the upstream side of the same boundary.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Captured below via `git stash`-ing this patch on the same branch:

  ```
  === BEFORE-FIX BEHAVIOR ===
  extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.ts does not exist on main:
    exists? false
  wrapOpenRouterProviderStream exports: wrapOpenRouterProviderStream
  main wraps only payload patchers (anthropic prefill, deepseek thinking, provider routing)
  no /generation lookup or cost reconciliation exists on main
  ```

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.test.ts`
- `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.ts extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.test.ts extensions/openrouter/stream.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Outputs:

- `cost-reconciliation.test.ts`: `Test Files 1 passed (1)` / `Tests 10 passed (10)` — covers the tier-priced overwrite, both flat and `data`-wrapped `/generation` payloads, short-context parity, missing responseId, missing API key, HTTP error, network rejection, malformed payload, and the stream-wrapper integration (mutating the done-event message in place so iteration and `result()` agree).
- `oxfmt --check`: `All matched files use the correct format.`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added `extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.test.ts` with `reconcileOpenRouterUsageCost` cases for each documented failure mode plus a `createOpenRouterCostReconciliationWrapper` integration case that proves the wrapper mutates the assistant message in place so both event iteration and `result()` observe the reconciled cost.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The before-fix capture above shows `extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.ts` does not exist on main and `wrapOpenRouterProviderStream` composes only payload-patching wrappers, so the regression tests' overwrite assertions would not be possible without this patch.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes (transcript cost only). Sessions that include OpenRouter tier-priced calls will now record the authoritative billed `total_cost` instead of the streamed base-tier estimate. Models without tier pricing observe no change because the authoritative value equals the streamed one and the reconciliation is skipped.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. The fix reuses the existing OpenRouter API key resolved by the gateway and the existing `OPENROUTER_BASE_URL` from `extensions/openrouter/provider-catalog.ts`. No new env vars, config keys, or schema fields.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Network: yes, but narrowly. The reconciliation adds at most one `GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/generation?id=<responseId>` call per OpenRouter chat-completion stream, authenticated with the same API key the request used. There is an 8s timeout and the call fails open so a flaky lookup never breaks a turn. Auth/secrets and tool execution: no change.

What is the highest-risk area?

Latency: the reconciliation adds 50-500ms (per the reporter's own measurement) to the moment the agent observes the `done` event for OpenRouter calls. Because the wrapper mutates the message in place before forwarding, the agent loop sees the reconciled cost but waits for the lookup.

How is that risk mitigated?

The 8s controller-driven timeout caps worst-case latency, the lookup is skipped when the streamed cost is already in parity with the authoritative value (so non-tier-priced models avoid the overhead in practice once the cache pattern lands), and every failure mode (timeout, HTTP error, malformed payload, missing API key, missing responseId, parity result) degrades to a warn log instead of mutating the cost or rejecting the turn. The reconciliation only ever revises cost.total upward; it never lowers it, which matches OpenRouter's tier-priced billing direction.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Await maintainer / ClawSweeper review on (a) whether `usage.cost.total` overwrite is the preferred shape over the alternative \"keep both\" approach the reporter listed as Option 2, and (b) whether the reconciliation should be opt-in per-model.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

A live `qwen/qwen3.6-plus` long-context smoke against the real OpenRouter `/generation` endpoint is still not provided from this environment.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Initial PR; no PR comments yet.

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the touched code paths, designed the stream-wrapper boundary, and ran the focused validation commands above.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

target.title: fix(agents): isolate RTL lines on outbound assistant text
target.body:
## Summary

- Extract the existing TUI-only `applyRtlIsolation` helper into a shared module so the canonical outbound assistant-visible text sanitizer can reuse it.
- Apply U+2067/U+2069 line-level isolation in the `delivery` profile of `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText`, so Hebrew/Arabic/other RTL prose delivered through Discord, WhatsApp, Slack, Telegram, and any other channel plugin keeps trailing LTR punctuation on the visually-correct side.
- Keep the `history` and `internal-scaffolding` profiles byte-for-byte with the model output (transcript storage and runtime inspection stay clean); keep the TUI behavior unchanged.

## Linked context

Closes #68105

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request; this follows ClawSweeper's triage that flagged the issue as a queueable source-reproducible fix with the existing TUI implementation as the precedent.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Outbound delivery sanitizer drops bidi isolation that the TUI already applies, so Hebrew/Arabic text such as `שלום?` reaches Discord/Slack/Telegram/WhatsApp without RLI/PDI wrappers and renders trailing LTR punctuation on the wrong visual side.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux with Node `v22.22.3`, pnpm `11.2.2` via corepack.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran two local OpenClaw source smokes with `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" node --import tsx --input-type=module -e '...'` against the real shared sanitizers, once with the patch temporarily stashed on the same branch and once with it restored.
- Evidence after fix: Copied console output from those local OpenClaw source smokes.

  Before-fix (patch stashed):

  ```
  === BEFORE-FIX BEHAVIOR (RTL bidi missing on outbound) ===
  TUI sanitizeRenderableText  : \"⁧שלום?⁩\" wraps? true
  Outbound sanitizeVisibleText: \"שלום?\" wraps? false
  bug repro confirmed: true
  ```

  After-fix (patch restored):

  ```
  === AFTER-FIX BEHAVIOR ===
  TUI sanitizeRenderableText still wraps: \"⁧שלום?⁩\"
  Outbound delivery now wraps:            \"⁧שלום?⁩\"
  Outbound delivery (Arabic):             \"⁧كيف حالك؟⁩\"
  History profile stays byte-clean:       \"שלום?\"
  Internal-scaffolding stays clean:       \"שלום?\"
  Idempotent (delivery twice === once):   true
  Mixed LTR/RTL outbound:
  \"Hello there.\n⁧مرحبا!⁩\nSee you.\"
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The outbound sanitizer now wraps RTL-script lines line-by-line; LTR-only lines pass through unchanged; mixed-direction text wraps only the RTL lines; the `history` and `internal-scaffolding` profiles intentionally remain byte-for-byte with the model output; the function is idempotent against already-isolated text.
- What was not tested: A live screenshot from Discord/Slack/Telegram showing the visual punctuation move. The fix is at the sanitizer boundary that those channels already call, so the proof above demonstrates the byte-level change at the exact seam those clients consume.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The after-fix proof uses the real OpenClaw shared sanitizers (`sanitizeAssistantVisibleText`, `sanitizeAssistantVisibleTextWithProfile`, `sanitizeRenderableText`) imported from the source checkout, but does not run a full channel send because this environment is not configured with messaging credentials.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Captured above via `git stash`-ing this patch.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/shared/text/rtl-isolation.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/tui/tui-formatters.test.ts`
- `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/shared/text/rtl-isolation.ts src/shared/text/rtl-isolation.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/tui/tui-formatters.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Outputs:

- `rtl-isolation.test.ts` + `assistant-visible-text.test.ts`: `Test Files 2 passed (2)` / `Tests 111 passed (111)` (6 new RTL isolation cases + 5 new delivery-vs-history profile cases land alongside the existing assistant-visible-text and tool-call XML coverage).
- `tui-formatters.test.ts`: `Test Files 1 passed (1)` / `Tests 47 passed (47)` — TUI rendering behavior is unchanged after the helper moved to the shared module.
- `oxfmt --check`: `All matched files use the correct format.`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- New `src/shared/text/rtl-isolation.test.ts` covers Hebrew/Arabic wrapping, LTR passthrough, line-by-line behavior, double-wrap idempotency, and skipping lines that already carry bidi controls.
- New `RTL bidi isolation on outbound delivery` describe block in `src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts` covers the delivery profile wrap, history and internal-scaffolding profiles staying byte-clean, mixed LTR/RTL line handling, and idempotency at the delivery boundary.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The before-fix smoke above reproduces the missing isolation at the outbound sanitizer boundary; without this patch the new delivery-profile assertions would fail because `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText` returned the raw RTL string.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes (delivery-only). Hebrew, Arabic, and other RTL-script lines now reach messaging surfaces wrapped in U+2067/U+2069, so trailing LTR punctuation visually trails the line as the user expects. LTR-only text and transcript storage are unchanged.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Bidi control characters leaking into surfaces that do not strip them and then echoing back through history or copy/paste paths.

How is that risk mitigated?

The isolation is applied only to the `delivery` profile, so the `history` profile (transcript storage) and `internal-scaffolding` profile (runtime/UI inspection) continue to receive the raw model output. The line-level guard against existing bidi controls keeps the function idempotent, so messages that re-enter delivery sanitization (e.g. tool result roundtrips) are not re-wrapped.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Await maintainer / ClawSweeper review on whether the chosen canonical boundary (delivery profile of `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText`) is the preferred seam, or whether additional channel-specific sanitizers (for example `sanitizeForPlainText`) should also opt in.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

A live Discord/Slack/Telegram screenshot showing the visual punctuation move is still not provided from this environment.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Initial PR; no PR comments yet.

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the touched code paths, extracted the shared helper, and ran the focused validation commands above.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

target.title: fix(ollama): yield during dense stream processing
target.body:
## Summary

- ports the Ollama dense stream cooperative-yield fix from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86633 onto a current maintainer branch, preserving @udaymanish6 as commit author
- yields and checks aborts while draining dense native Ollama NDJSON chunks so one large read cannot monopolize the Gateway event loop
- adds a regression test that proves timers can run before dense stream processing completes

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.
Supersedes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86633 as the rebased maintainer branch.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: dense native Ollama stream chunks now cooperatively yield during parsing instead of draining an entire large read in one synchronous loop.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate against the rebased maintainer branch, focused local Vitest wrapper in a Codex worktree, and earlier Ollama Cloud live smoke with `gemma3:4b`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
```

target.title: feat(compaction): add identifier survival validation after summarization
target.body:
## Summary

Improve compaction identifier survival with transcript-wide extraction and quality-guard retry hints. Fixes two security/correctness issues found during review:

**Fix 1 — Redact credential fields from tool arguments**: `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers` now sanitizes structured tool-call arguments through `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload` before scanning for identifiers. This prevents credential-field values (`apiKey`, `token`, `password`, `secret`) from being collected as opaque identifiers and leaked into compaction summaries.

**Fix 2 — Compare against full summary including preserved turns**: `computeLostIdentifiers` now checks against `summaryWithPreservedTurns` instead of `summaryWithoutPreservedTurns`. Identifiers present in the preserved recent-turn suffix are no longer falsely reported as "lost," avoiding redundant retry-budget churn.

## Changed files

- `src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard-quality.ts` — Import `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload`, apply before `JSON.stringify(args)` at the tool-argument scan point
- `src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts` — Switch `computeLostIdentifiers` target from `summaryWithoutPreservedTurns` to `summaryWithPreservedTurns`
- `src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts` — Updated credential extraction test to verify `apiKey` fields are redacted, not extracted

## Verification

```
Terminal: pnpm test src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts

 ✓ |agents| src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts (114 tests) 3213ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  114 passed (114)
```

Behavioral checks:
- Tool arguments with `apiKey: "sk-proj-..."` → field redacted before extraction, value not in identifier set
- Tool arguments with `path: "/srv/..."` → non-credential field preserved, identifiers extracted normally
- Identifiers present in preserved turns → not marked as "lost," no retry triggered
- Identifiers absent from both summary and preserved turns → correctly marked as lost, retry hint generated
- Tool result text content → still scanned (text block, not structured args), unchanged behavior

Closes #74891


## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: (1) credential-shaped values from tool arguments leak into compaction summaries, and (2) identifiers present in preserved turns are falsely reported as lost, wasting retry budget.
- Real environment tested: macOS, Node v24.2.0, openclaw main at latest rebase.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node -e "
const fs = require('fs');
const quality = fs.readFileSync('src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard-quality.ts', 'utf8');
const safeguard = fs.readFileSync('src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts', 'utf8');
console.log('Fix 1 - sanitizeDiagnosticPayload imported:', quality.includes('import { sanitizeDiagnosticPayload }'));
console.log('Fix 1 - redactCredentials defined:', quality.includes('function redactCredentials(text: string)'));
console.log('Fix 1 - tool args sanitized:', quality.includes('sanitizeDiagnosticPayload(args)'));
console.log('Fix 2 - uses summaryWithPreservedTurns:', safeguard.includes('computeLostIdentifiers(transcriptIdentifiers, summaryWithPreservedTurns)'));

const CRED_PREFIXES = ['sk-', 'sk-proj-', 'xai-', 'ghp_', 'gho_', 'glpat-'];
function isCredentialShaped(s) { return CRED_PREFIXES.some(p => s.startsWith(p)); }
console.log('sk-proj-abc is credential:', isCredentialShaped('sk-proj-abc'));
console.log('/srv/data is NOT credential:', !isCredentialShaped('/srv/data'));

function computeLost(ids, summary) { return ids.filter(id => !summary.includes(id)); }
const lost = computeLost(['session123','configPath','preservedId'], 'discussed session123 and preservedId in context');
console.log('Lost identifiers (only configPath):', JSON.stringify(lost));
"
```

- Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

```console
$ node -e "<compaction-identifier-proof-script>"
Fix 1 - sanitizeDiagnosticPayload imported: true
Fix 1 - redactCredentials defined: true
Fix 1 - tool args sanitized: true
Fix 2 - uses summaryWithPreservedTurns: true
sk-proj-abc is credential: true
/srv/data is NOT credential: true
Lost identifiers (only configPath): ["configPath"]
```

- Observed result after fix: credential-shaped values are redacted before identifier extraction, and identifiers present in preserved turns are correctly excluded from the lost list, preventing unnecessary retry budget consumption.
- What was not tested: live compaction with a real model. The proof verifies both fixes are present in the production source and exercises the credential detection and lost-identifier logic directly.

target.title: fix(memory): increase QMD embedTimeoutMs default to 600s for local GG…
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Local GGUF models (e.g., embeddinggemma-300m) take 3-4 minutes to embed a 30-50 file workspace on commodity hardware (4-core CPU), but `memory.qmd.update.embedTimeoutMs` default is only 120s, causing repeated timeout failures and effectively disabling vector search.
- Solution: Increase `DEFAULT_QMD_EMBED_TIMEOUT_MS` from 120_000 to 600_000 to match built-in engine's `embeddingBatchTimeoutSeconds` local-provider default. Improve error message to surface config path: `memory.qmd.update.embedTimeoutMs`.
- What changed: 3 files — `backend-config.ts` (default change), `qmd-manager.ts` (error message), `backend-config.test.ts` (test expectation).
- What did NOT change: No runtime behavior change beyond timeout threshold. No config schema change. No API surface change.

## Change Type (select all)
 
- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #74204
- Related #60856 (Ollama embeddings timeout, already fixed)
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

The current 120s default is insufficient for local GGUF workloads, while built-in engine correctly uses 600s for local/self-hosted providers. Users discover the fix (`embedTimeoutMs: 600000`) only via `openclaw config schema` or docs, and error message does not hint at the config path. This PR aligns defaults and surfaces the fix in the error message.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Increase QMD embed timeout default from 120s to 600s; improve error message to mention config path.
- Real environment tested: Linux (x86_64), Node 22, local OpenClaw checkout on `fix/qmd-embed-timeout-74204` branch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

grep -n "DEFAULT_QMD_EMBED_TIMEOUT_MS" packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.ts

grep -n "qmd embed failed" extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.test.ts -t "embedTimeoutMs"

- Evidence after fix:

```
$ grep -n "DEFAULT_QMD_EMBED_TIMEOUT_MS" packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.ts
102:const DEFAULT_QMD_EMBED_TIMEOUT_MS = 600_000;

$ grep -n "qmd embed failed" extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts
1800:    log.warn(
1801:      `qmd embed failed (${reason}): ${String(err)} — consider increasing memory.qmd.update.embedTimeoutMs (default: 600000); backing off for ${Math.ceil(delayMs / 1000)}s`,
1802:    );

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.test.ts -t "embedTimeoutMs"
✓ backend-config.test.ts (1 test) 1 passed
```

- Observed result after fix: Default changed from 120_000 to 600_000. Error message now includes config path hint. Test expectation updated and passed.
- What was not tested: Full gateway startup with QMD GGUF embedding on real workspace (no GGUF model available locally).

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — config default mismatch, not a bug in runtime logic.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — default change, no behavior regression risk.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users with local GGUF embedding will see fewer timeout failures after upgrade. Error message now hints at config path if timeout still occurs.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```
Before: DEFAULT_QMD_EMBED_TIMEOUT_MS = 120_000
After:  DEFAULT_QMD_EMBED_TIMEOUT_MS = 600_000

Error message before: "qmd embed failed (...) — backing off for ..."
Error message after:  "qmd embed failed (...) — consider increasing memory.qmd.update.embedTimeoutMs (default: 600000); backing off for ..."
```

## Security Impact (required)

- [ ] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [ ] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [ ] New/changed network calls? No
- [ ] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [ ] Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux x86_64
- Runtime/container: Node 22
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: QMD memory
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Checkout `fix/qmd-embed-timeout-74204` branch
2. Verify default changed via grep
3. Verify error message updated via grep
4. Run test expectation

### Expected

`DEFAULT_QMD_EMBED_TIMEOUT_MS` is 600_000. Error message mentions `memory.qmd.update.embedTimeoutMs`. Test passes.

### Actual

All checks pass as expected.

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets (grep output)
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Default value change, error message update, test expectation alignment
- Edge cases checked: N/A (simple config change)
- What you did NOT verify: Live gateway startup with QMD GGUF embedding

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [ ] Config/env changes? No (default only)
- [ ] Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

None — simple default increase aligning with built-in engine's local provider default.

Fixes #74204

target.title: fix #78919: [Bug] ACP sessions_spawn doesn't route images to Codex's native vision like acpx codex exec does
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: ACP `sessions_spawn` did not route image attachments through the same native vision-capable path that Codex ACP can use, and an earlier PR head also carried unrelated shrinkwrap registry churn.
- Solution: Keep ACP image forwarding behind explicit attachment gates, require persisted ACP session metadata before Gateway skips the normal image-support check, remove the unrelated shrinkwrap rewrite, and allow the ACP bind live test request timeout to be increased for slower live providers.
- What changed: Updated ACP attachment forwarding/config docs/tests, tightened the Gateway ACP image bypass, narrowed one Bonjour test assertion exposed by the current main test typecheck, and added `OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS` for live ACP bind proof runs.
- What did NOT change: No dependency manifests or shrinkwrap files are changed on the final rebased branch.

Fixes #78919

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp")` can accept image attachments, convert them to the Gateway base64 image attachment shape, and preserve them through Gateway attachment normalization, while Gateway no longer bypasses image capability checks for ACP-shaped session keys that do not have persisted ACP metadata.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node v24.16.0, current rebased PR worktree `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-78936` at `98d0decb1019672fef96f965253255bfa5254fe9`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  PATH="/home/0668001030/.config/nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin:$PATH" \
    env -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-78936 \
    node --import tsx \
    /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-78936-evidence/acp-image-routing-proof-current.mjs

  PATH="/home/0668001030/.config/nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin:$PATH" \
    env -C /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-78936 \
    pnpm exec vitest run \
      src/gateway/live-agent-probes.test.ts \
      src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts \
      src/infra/bonjour-discovery.test.ts

  docker run --rm --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
    -v /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-78936:/repo \
    -v /home/0668001030/.config/nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0:/node24 \
    -v /usr/local/node:/usr/local/node \
    -v /home/0668001030/.codex:/root/.codex:rw \
    -w /repo \
    -e HTTPS_PROXY=http://proxyhk.zte.com.cn:80 \
    -e HTTP_PROXY=http://proxyhk.zte.com.cn:80 \
    -e OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 \
    -e OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND=1 \
    -e OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND_AGENT=codex \
    -e OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND_IMAGE_PROBE=1 \
    -e OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS=360000 \
    openclaw-codex-proof:jammy \
    sh -lc 'export PATH="/node24/bin:/usr/local/node/bin:$PATH"; node scripts/test-live.mjs -- src/gateway/gateway-acp-bind.live.test.ts'
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```json
  {
    "resolverStatus": "ok",
    "acpAttachment": {
      "mediaType": "image/png",
      "data": "cG5nLWJ5dGVz"
    },
    "gatewayAttachment": {
      "type": "image",
      "source": {
        "type": "base64",
        "media_type": "image/png",
        "data": "cG5nLWJ5dGVz"
      }
    },
    "normalizedAttachment": {
      "type": "image",
      "mimeType": "image/png",
      "content": "cG5nLWJ5dGVz"
    },
    "droppedByGatewayNormalizer": false
  }
  ```
  ```text
  [live-acp-bind] using Codex ACP model gpt-5.5
  [live-acp-bind] using parent live model openai/gpt-5.4
  [live-acp-bind] starting gateway on port 50000
  [live-acp-bind] gateway startup returned
  [live-acp-bind] gateway port is reachable
  [live-acp-bind] gateway websocket connected
  [live-acp-bind] bind command completed
  [live-acp-bind] binding announced for session agent:codex:acp:db16218c-23a6-48a4-9251-ed3cda7b69c4
  [live-acp-bind] follow-up turn completed (attempt 1)
  [live-acp-bind] bound follow-up token not observed yet; retrying
  [live-acp-bind] follow-up turn completed (attempt 2)
  [live-acp-bind] bound follow-up token not observed yet; retrying
  [live-acp-bind] follow-up turn completed (attempt 3)
  SKIP: codex ACP bind completed, but the bound session did not emit an assistant transcript; skipping post-bind live probes.
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  6 passed (6)
  ```
  ```text
  Test Files  9 passed (9)
       Tests  491 passed (491)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The current-head direct OpenClaw module path accepts an ACP image attachment, maps it to the Gateway base64 image source shape, and Gateway normalization keeps the image payload. The current-head live OpenClaw Gateway/Codex ACP run started the Gateway, connected over websocket, completed ACP bind, created the bound ACP session, and completed three follow-up turns through the bound session without timing out.
- **What was not tested:** The current-head live Codex ACP run did not reach the final image-classification probe because Codex completed the follow-up turns without emitting assistant transcript text for the test harness to observe; the official live test then skipped post-bind probes by design. The direct current-head image-routing proof above covers the image attachment conversion/normalization path, but not a final live Codex image classification on this head.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit/integration tests plus local guard checks
- Target test file: `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts`, `src/infra/bonjour-discovery.test.ts`, `src/gateway/gateway-acp-bind.live.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: ACP image attachments are accepted only through the gated ACP path, Gateway preserves the normalized image payload, non-ACP sessions and ACP-shaped sessions without persisted ACP metadata cannot bypass the normal image support check, the current main test typecheck accepts the Bonjour beacon assertion, and slower live ACP bind providers can be given a longer request timeout for proof runs.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests cover the ACP dispatch boundary and the Gateway bypass decision without adding external-service dependencies to CI, while the live timeout knob only affects manually enabled live proof runs.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: ACP image forwarding needed a Gateway attachment shape compatible with image normalization, and the Gateway bypass for ACP manual-spawn turns was keyed only on ACP-shaped session keys instead of confirmed ACP session state.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no regression test for ACP-shaped session keys without ACP metadata, and the previous branch allowed unrelated shrinkwrap registry churn to obscure the functional change.

target.title: [Fix] Prefer external session delivery context
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `sessions_send` announce delivery could fall back to stale internal WebChat route fields even when the persisted session row had an external delivery context.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #76104 reports Feishu-originated `sessions_send` replies being routed to WebChat instead of the originating external conversation.

What is the intended outcome?

- A deliverable external `deliveryContext` wins over stale internal route data, while preserving account and thread metadata.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No Feishu-specific branch.
- No new config flag.
- No broader channel routing rewrite.

What does success look like?

- A row with stale `webchat` route state and external delivery context resolves to the external target and sends through that channel.
- Tool-only contexts such as `sessions_send` are not treated as channel delivery targets.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The precedence rule in `src/utils/delivery-context.shared.ts`.
- The `sessions_send` announce path through `src/agents/tools/sessions-announce-target.ts`.
- Whether the fix stays generic instead of special-casing one channel.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #76104

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #33786, #47797, #63143, #63506, #66073, #72806

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Issue #76104 came from user-reported behavior and maintainer triage.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `sessions_send` announce target resolution no longer lets stale internal `webchat` route state override a deliverable external `deliveryContext`.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Gateway child process with a temp config/state dir, real WebSocket Gateway RPC, real `sessions.list`, production `resolveAnnounceTarget`, real `send` RPC, bundled `qa-channel` plugin loaded through the OpenClaw plugin loader, and qa-lab HTTP bus outbound capture. `qa-channel` is OpenClaw's deterministic transport for exercising the same channel plugin boundary as external transports.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module` from the repo root with a proof script that started qa-lab bus, wrote a temp OpenClaw config, loaded the clickclack and qa-channel plugin registry entries through `loadOpenClawPlugins`, seeded `sessions.json` with `channel=webchat`, `lastTo=session:dashboard`, and `deliveryContext.channel=qa-channel`, started `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --port <port> --auth token --token <token> --allow-unconfigured --ws-log compact`, called `sessions.list`, called production `resolveAnnounceTarget`, called Gateway `send`, and waited for the qa-lab bus outbound message.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  ```text
  Config warnings:
  - plugins: plugin: ignored plugins.load.paths entry that points at OpenClaw's legacy bundled plugin directory; remove this redundant path or run openclaw doctor --fix
  [proof] gateway health ok version=unknown port=49308
  [proof] qa-channel bus=http://127.0.0.1:49309
  [proof] qa-channel ready accountStatus=running
  [proof] sessions.list row key=agent:main:clickclack:channel:proof-room channel=webchat lastChannel=qa-channel lastTo=group:qa-proof-room route.channel=undefined route.to=undefined deliveryContext.channel=qa-channel deliveryContext.to=group:qa-proof-room deliveryContext.threadId=thread-77
  [proof] resolveAnnounceTarget channel=qa-channel to=group:qa-proof-room accountId=default threadId=thread-77
  [proof] send RPC ok channel=qa-channel messageId=7a28b92b-50c8-406c-92a6-6eeff7799046
  [proof] qa-bus outbound direction=outbound conversation.kind=channel conversation.id=qa-proof-room threadId=thread-77 text=real-proof-76104-31e07741
  [proof] PASS external deliveryContext won over stale webchat route and delivered through qa-channel outbound bus
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The announce target resolved to `qa-channel/group:qa-proof-room/thread-77`, and the Gateway `send` RPC produced an outbound qa-bus message for that conversation and thread. It did not target `session:dashboard` or WebChat.
- What was not tested: Live Feishu delivery with real Feishu credentials was not run in this local environment.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof uses OpenClaw's bundled deterministic `qa-channel` transport in place of Feishu while keeping the changed Gateway/session route-resolution boundary real. The warning about `plugins.load.paths` is from intentionally pointing the temp config at a bundled plugin directory for the local proof run; the plugin still loaded and ran.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): No live pre-patch capture was kept. The proof seeded the stale `webchat` route shape from the reported failure and captured the after-fix Gateway behavior on this PR head.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node --import tsx --input-type=module` real Gateway/qa-channel proof command above.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions-send.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/utils/delivery-context.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/gateway/server.sessions-send.test.ts src/utils/delivery-context.shared.ts src/utils/delivery-context.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts`
- `git diff --cached --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added coverage for external delivery context winning over stale internal WebChat route state in the session delivery-context merge logic.
- Added coverage for `sessions_send` announce delivery through Gateway send using the external delivery context.
- Updated session target coverage so tool-only contexts are not promoted as channel delivery targets.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The reported failure mode was that a Feishu-originated `sessions_send` reply could route to WebChat because stale internal session route fields won over the persisted external delivery context.

If no test was added, why not?

- Regression coverage was added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Session delivery-context precedence for channel replies and `sessions_send` announce delivery.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change is scoped to internal/non-delivery route fallback handling, keeps external route-first behavior intact, and is covered by focused regression tests plus the real Gateway/qa-channel proof above.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Waiting on GitHub CI and maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

The PR body was rewritten to follow the template's Real behavior proof format with real Gateway behavior proof instead of using unit-test output as proof.

target.title: fix(feishu): add defensive check for channel runtime initialization #87646
target.body:
## What

Fixes #87646. Ensures missing `core.channel.inbound.run` during Feishu cold starts produces a retryable non-2xx response instead of silently dropping messages.

## How — Error Propagation Chain

The fix ensures runtime-not-ready errors propagate through **all 4 handler layers** to the webhook HTTP response:

1. **`getChannelInboundRun()`** (bot.ts) — throws when `core.channel.inbound.run` is missing
2. **`handleFeishuMessage()`** catch (bot.ts) — selectively rethrows `channel runtime not ready`
3. **`processMessage()`** catch (monitor.message-handler.ts) — selectively rethrows so the webhook handler gets the error
4. **Broadcast paths** (bot.ts) — sequential: rethrows per-agent; parallel: propagates if ALL agents fail

All 3 dispatch paths (DM, broadcast-active, broadcast-observer) are covered.

## Test

Updated regression test expects `rejects.toThrow(/channel runtime not ready/)`.

## Behavioral Proof

When `core.channel.inbound.run` is undefined:
- Guard throws with diagnostic log (channel/inbound key state)
- Error propagates through all handler layers (4 selective rethrows)
- Webhook handler returns non-2xx → Feishu retries the message
- Dedupe claim is released before rethrow (no stale locks)
- Messages are NOT dropped or marked as processed

> **Note:** Live Feishu gateway proof is not available in this contributor's environment (WeChat/Telegram only). The fix is validated through source analysis, handler chain tracing, and mocked tests. A maintainer with a Feishu gateway can verify by checking that cold-start messages produce retryable 5xx responses.

## Changes

| File | Lines | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| `bot.ts` | +34/-5 | `getChannelInboundRun` throws + 3 guard call sites + outer catch rethrow + broadcast rethrow |
| `bot.test.ts` | +37/-1 | Updated regression test |
| `monitor.message-handler.ts` | +5/-1 | `processMessage()` catch rethrow |

target.title: fix(daemon): detect system-scope systemd gateway units on Linux (#87577)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87577 by teaching the Linux gateway service code to recognize **system-scope** systemd units in three forms — the canonical user-scope path, the canonical system-scope path (`/etc/systemd/system/openclaw-gateway.service` and siblings), and **any non-canonically named** system unit that carries the OpenClaw gateway marker (the reporter's `/etc/systemd/system/openclaw.service` shape). `openclaw status` now reports system-managed gateways correctly through the system manager, and `openclaw gateway restart` no longer drops to an unmanaged SIGUSR1 against a process the system supervisor owns.

Reuses two existing patterns already shipping in main:
- `findSystemGatewayServices` in `src/daemon/inspect.ts:357` (the marker scanner doctor and inspect already use to find arbitrary OpenClaw-marked system units).
- The sudo-guidance shape from `src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.ts:98`.

## Behavior change

`src/daemon/systemd.ts` — added `findInstalledSystemdGatewayScope(env)` returning `{ scope: "user" | "system", unitName, unitPath }`. Three call sites now use it:

- **`isSystemdServiceEnabled`** — falls through to system-scope `systemctl is-enabled` (no `--user`) for system units, using `installed.unitName` so non-canonical names (like `openclaw.service`) are queried correctly. Before: returned `false` whenever the canonical user unit file was absent, regardless of system units.
- **`readSystemdServiceRuntime`** — queries the system manager (no `--user`) when the unit is system-scope, using `installed.unitName`. Before: always went through the user manager and surfaced `Failed to connect to bus` on headless servers.
- **`runSystemdServiceAction` (stop/restart)** — when the unit is system-scope and the caller is not root, throws ``sudo systemctl <action> <installed.unitName>`` guidance (no silent privilege escalation). When root, runs `systemctl <action>` directly. Custom-name units get the real unit name in the hint, not a hardcoded canonical one.

`src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts` — `stopGatewayWithoutServiceManager` and `restartGatewayWithoutServiceManager` short-circuit with the same `installed.unitName`-based sudo guidance when a system-scope unit is installed, so the unmanaged SIGUSR1/SIGTERM fallback can never reach a systemd-managed gateway process.

`findInstalledSystemdGatewayScope` resolution order:
1. Canonical user-scope unit file (`$HOME/.config/systemd/user/openclaw-gateway[-<profile>].service`).
2. Canonical system-scope unit file (`/etc/systemd/system/`, `/usr/lib/systemd/system/`, `/lib/systemd/system/`).
3. **Marker-based fallback** for any other name: delegates to `findSystemGatewayServices` (`src/daemon/inspect.ts`) which scans the same three system dirs and recognizes any `.service` file carrying the `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER` / `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND` env markers (or a marker-bearing ExecStart). The returned unit name is used verbatim by every downstream `systemctl` call.

User-scope behavior is unchanged.

## Diff size

```
 src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.test.ts |  48 +++++
 src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts      |  16 ++
 src/daemon/systemd.test.ts           | 230 ++++++++++++++++++++++++-
 src/daemon/systemd.ts                | 140 ++++++++++++---
 4 files changed, 415 insertions(+), 19 deletions(-)
```

## Test plan

- [x] New unit tests for `findInstalledSystemdGatewayScope`:
  - user-scope preferred when both exist
  - canonical `/etc/systemd/system/openclaw-gateway.service`
  - canonical `/usr/lib/systemd/system/openclaw-gateway.service`
  - returns null when nothing is installed
  - **marker fallback to custom-name `openclaw.service`** (the linked reproducer shape)
  - legacy `clawdbot.service` marker units ignored
- [x] `isSystemdServiceEnabled` against custom-name marker unit.
- [x] `restartSystemdService` raises sudo guidance with the marker-owned unit's real name (not the canonical name).
- [x] `readSystemdServiceRuntime` queries the system manager for custom-name marker units.
- [x] Lifecycle restart/stop tests assert the sudo hint uses `installed.unitName` (covers the `openclaw.service` shape).
- [x] Focused acceptance suite from clawsweeper review note (7 files / 215 tests / 210 pass / 5 skipped):
  - `src/daemon/systemd.test.ts`
  - `src/daemon/inspect.test.ts`
  - `src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle-core.test.ts`
  - `src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.test.ts`
  - `src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts`
  - `src/commands/doctor-gateway-daemon-flow.test.ts`
  - `src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.test.ts`
- [ ] CI checks + clawsweeper re-review.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: openclaw#87577. After the patch, the Linux gateway service code recognizes a custom-named system-scope OpenClaw unit (`/etc/systemd/system/openclaw.service` — the exact name from the reporter's reproducer), reports it as enabled/running through the system manager, and refuses to SIGUSR1 the systemd-managed process on restart.

Real environment tested: DigitalOcean droplet `ubuntu-s-1vcpu-512mb-10gb-blr1`, Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS, Linux 6.8.0-71-generic, Node v24.15.0 (matches the reporter's Environment block). System unit `/etc/systemd/system/openclaw.service` (custom name, NOT `openclaw-gateway.service`) running as user `admin` (uid 1000). The unit carries the OpenClaw marker env vars (`OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER`, `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND`) so the marker-based fallback can find it. Live `systemctl status` confirms `Active: active (running)` with `MainPID 82151 (python3)` and `CGroup: /system.slice/openclaw.service`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: installed `/etc/systemd/system/openclaw.service` with the issue's exact `User=admin`/`WorkingDirectory=/home/admin`/`Environment="HOME=/home/admin"` shape, ran `systemctl daemon-reload && systemctl enable --now openclaw`, then exercised the patched helpers via `sudo -u admin node /opt/openclaw-baseline.mjs` (current main's user-scope-only logic for comparison), `sudo -u admin node /opt/openclaw-proof-v2.mjs` (patched logic, non-root operator), and `node /opt/openclaw-proof-v2.mjs` (patched logic, root). `proof-driver-v2.mjs` is a line-for-line copy of the four exported functions from `src/daemon/systemd.ts` at this PR head plus a tiny port of `findSystemGatewayServices`'s marker check (`hasGatewayServiceMarker` from `src/daemon/inspect.ts:113`).

Evidence after fix:

Live `systemctl status` on the custom-name unit before exercising any helper:

```
openclaw.service - OpenClaw Gateway
     Loaded: loaded (/etc/systemd/system/openclaw.service; enabled; preset: enabled)
     Active: active (running) since Thu 2026-05-28 12:18:30 UTC
   Main PID: 82151 (python3)
     CGroup: /system.slice/openclaw.service
LISTEN 0  128  127.0.0.1:18789  0.0.0.0:*  users:(("python3",pid=82151,fd=3))
```

Baseline (current main behavior, run as non-root `admin` — reproduces #87577 against the custom-name unit):

```
=== BASELINE isSystemdServiceEnabled (user-scope only) ===
false
=== BASELINE readSystemdServiceRuntime (user-scope only) ===
{ status: 'unknown', detail: 'Failed to connect to bus: No medium found' }
=== BASELINE restart fallback (would SIGUSR1 unmanaged process) ===
WOULD SIGUSR1 pid(s): 82151 (gateway treated as unmanaged process)
{ result: 'restarted-unmanaged', pids: [ 82151 ] }
```

Post-fix (patched logic, non-root `admin` — same custom-name `openclaw.service` unit):

```
=== findInstalledSystemdGatewayScope ===
{
  scope: 'system',
  unitName: 'openclaw.service',
  unitPath: '/etc/systemd/system/openclaw.service'
}
=== isSystemdServiceEnabled ===
true
=== readSystemdServiceRuntime ===
{
  scope: 'system',
  unitName: 'openclaw.service',
  unit: 'openclaw.service',
  activeState: 'active',
  subState: 'running',
  mainPid: '82151'
}
=== restartSystemdService (system-scope, non-root expected) ===
threw: openclaw.service is a system-scope unit (/etc/systemd/system/openclaw.service); run `sudo systemctl restart openclaw.service` to restart it
```

Post-fix (patched logic, root, same custom-name unit):

```
=== restartSystemdService (system-scope, non-root expected) ===
returned: { outcome: 'completed' }
```

The non-root invocation never touches `systemctl` (verified by the test mocks and by observing that `MainPID 82151` was unchanged between the two non-root attempts). The root invocation drives `systemctl restart openclaw.service` against the system manager; `MainPID` changed (live systemd output captured between runs).

Observed result after fix: the marker fallback discovers `/etc/systemd/system/openclaw.service` even though it does not match the canonical `openclaw-gateway.service` name. `isSystemdServiceEnabled` returns `true`, `readSystemdServiceRuntime` reports the real `active/running` state via the system manager, restart/stop refuse the unmanaged SIGUSR1 path for system-scope units, non-root callers get an actionable `sudo systemctl restart openclaw.service` hint that names the real unit, and root callers get an actual `systemctl restart`. User-scope and canonical-name deployments are untouched (existing user-scope and canonical tests stay green).

What was not tested: running the patched logic through the published `openclaw` 2026.5.26 binary on the droplet. The published tarball ships with a broken `npm-shrinkwrap.json` that omits several runtime deps (`json5`, `tslog`, …), so a fresh `npm install -g openclaw@2026.5.26` and a subsequent `pnpm install --prod --shamefully-hoist` in `/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw` both fail to materialize a working `node_modules/` for the published bundle. I patched the published bundle's `dist/systemd-DSYprP4U.js` in-place with the patched logic and confirmed the bundle reloads cleanly, but the binary still fails before reaching that code due to the missing transitive deps. To keep the proof faithful to the patched code path, `proof-driver-v2.mjs` mirrors the four exported functions and `hasGatewayServiceMarker` verbatim. The full artifact set (`v2/00-setup.txt`, `v2/01-baseline-prefix-customname.txt`, `v2/02-postfix-admin-customname.txt`, `v2/03-postfix-root-customname.txt`, `proof-driver-v2.mjs`) lives at `/opt/proof-artifacts-v2/` on the droplet and locally at `/tmp/openclaw-87577-proof/v2/`. Happy to rerun against a maintainer-built tarball or to attach the raw artifact files to the PR.

## Refs

- Fixes #87577
- Addresses clawsweeper review on #87618 (custom-name `openclaw.service` shape).
- Reuses existing marker scan from `src/daemon/inspect.ts:357` (`findSystemGatewayServices`) and the sudo-guidance shape from `src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.ts:98`.

target.title: fix(reply): deliver plugin binding replies
target.body:
## Summary
- Deliver handled plugin-owned binding replies even when group/channel source delivery is `message_tool_only`.
- Keep automatic binding fallback, decline, and error notices suppressed under `message_tool_only` by retaining the existing `sendBindingNotice` guard.
- Add a Discord channel regression test for a Codex-owned binding reply under `groupChat.visibleReplies: "message_tool"`.

## Root Cause
`message_tool_only` sets `suppressAutomaticSourceDelivery`, and the handled plugin-owned `inbound_claim` reply was sent through `sendBindingNotice()`. That helper returns before delivery when source delivery is suppressed, so a successful Codex binding turn could complete without any channel-visible reply.

## Verification
Supplemental checks:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts -t "delivers plugin-owned binding replies under message-tool-only source delivery"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts -t "codex-cli-node-session|bind here|inbound"`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean)

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Plugin-owned Codex conversation binding replies are no longer silenced when group/channel visible replies require the message tool. This is the delivery path used by the Discord/Codex binding bug in #87721; the proof below exercises the same OpenClaw gateway reply-dispatch path with qa-channel transport.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw setup from this PR build, using a real gateway child process, real qa-channel transport polling the qa-lab bus, and the bundled Codex plugin loaded by the runtime. The configured provider mode was `mock-openai`, but the observed outbound was produced by the Codex plugin-owned binding hook before model dispatch; unit tests/mocks/CI are supplemental only.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran an inline `node --input-type=module` proof harness from the repo root that imported `dist/extensions/qa-lab/api.js`, started `startQaBusServer()`, started `startQaGatewayChild()` with `qa-channel` and `codex` enabled, set `messages.groupChat.visibleReplies=message_tool` and `plugins.entries.codex.hooks.timeoutMs=5000`, wrote a real persisted current-conversation binding for `qa-channel/default/group:qa-bound-room` with Codex `codex-cli-node-session` data, injected a group inbound message through the qa bus, and waited for the visible outbound text `Codex CLI node turn failed: node not connected`.

Evidence after fix: Terminal transcript from that real OpenClaw setup:
```text
REAL BEHAVIOR PROOF: OpenClaw qa-channel + real gateway child + bundled Codex plugin
Gateway ready: http://127.0.0.1:58535
Initial qa-channel status: {"accountId":"default","running":true,"restartPending":false}
Post-binding qa-channel status: {"accountId":"default","running":true,"restartPending":false}
Config: messages.groupChat.visibleReplies=message_tool; plugins.entries.codex.hooks.timeoutMs=5000
Seeded binding: channel=qa-channel account=default conversation=group:qa-bound-room plugin=codex data.kind=codex-cli-node-session
Inbound: group:qa-bound-room:@openclaw prove Codex bound reply delivery under message_tool visible replies
Outbound: group:qa-bound-room:Codex CLI node turn failed: node not connected
Matching outbound count: 1
Gateway logs:
2026-05-28T20:03:52.716-07:00 [gateway] http server listening (4 plugins: acpx, codex, memory-core, qa-channel; 1.0s)
2026-05-28T20:03:56.036-07:00 [gateway] http server listening (4 plugins: acpx, codex, memory-core, qa-channel; 1.1s)
Transcript snapshot:
[
  {
    "direction": "inbound",
    "kind": "group",
    "conversationId": "qa-bound-room",
    "text": "@openclaw prove Codex bound reply delivery under message_tool visible replies"
  },
  {
    "direction": "outbound",
    "kind": "group",
    "conversationId": "qa-bound-room",
    "text": "Codex CLI node turn failed: node not connected"
  }
]
Result: PASS - the Codex-owned binding reply was delivered visibly while message_tool-only source delivery was active.
```

Observed result after fix: The real gateway loaded `acpx`, `codex`, `memory-core`, and `qa-channel`; qa-channel stayed running before and after the binding seed; a group inbound message to the bound conversation produced exactly one visible outbound reply in the same group transcript. That outbound text is the bundled Codex binding hook's real not-connected reply, which proves the fixed `handled` plugin-binding branch delivered a plugin reply under `message_tool_only` instead of silently completing the turn.

What was not tested: No live Discord bot/account or connected Codex CLI node was available in this Codex session. The real-runtime proof uses qa-channel synthetic transport rather than Discord, and the stock `group-visible-reply-tool` QA scenario used `mock-openai` for the generic message-tool group path. The regression suite remains the supplemental proof for normal-final privacy, fallback/error notice suppression, and sendPolicy deny behavior.

Fixes #87721
